column1: Investigate and document the recurring dev → main squash-merge divergence pattern
column27:
Every Friday {{dev → main}} promotion exhibits the same pattern: the squash-merge of {{dev}} into {{main}} produces a "divergent histories" warning, and the cleanest path through is a back-merge PR ({{main → dev}} via its own merge commit) before the squash-merge to {{main}} resolves. This is currently treated as a standing operational quirk - known, expected, worked around manually each week - but it has not been formally diagnosed or documented in the repo, and it is not tracked anywhere as a ticket until now.

Root cause is suspected but not confirmed. The divergence almost certainly arises because:

* {{feat/* → dev}} PRs are squash-merged, which produces a single new commit on {{dev}} that is NOT a child of any of the feature-branch commits. The feature branch's commits never appear in {{main}}'s ancestor history.
* {{dev → main}} is then squash-merged again (per [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|smart-link]'s {{Enforce dev → main}} source-branch restriction and ADR 0006), producing a single squash commit on {{main}}.
* After the {{main}} squash, {{dev}}'s tip and {{main}}'s tip share no common commit ancestor with the just-squashed feature work, so {{git}} reports "histories diverge."
* The standard fix (back-merge {{main}} into {{dev}} via a non-squash merge commit) re-establishes the ancestry relationship at the cost of a merge commit on {{dev}}.

Net result: every Friday adds one back-merge commit to {{dev}}, and the pattern is operationally invisible if you just follow the runbook, but it accumulates noise in {{dev}}'s history and adds a step to every promotion that should not be necessary.

This ticket is to investigate, document, and either accept or fix.

Deliverables

* Investigate the actual git mechanics with the live repo. Reproduce the divergence on a throwaway branch pair and capture exactly which commits are diverging and why.
* Decide between three postures: (a) accept the pattern, document it explicitly in {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}'s "Friday promotion" section so it stops being treated as surprising; (b) fix the root cause by changing the merge strategy (e.g. require non-squash merge for {{dev → main}}, accept the noisier {{main}} log in exchange for clean ancestry); (c) fix the root cause via tooling (a script or pre-promotion check that auto-back-merges, removing the manual step).
* Capture the decision as an ADR ({{0007-dev-to-main-merge-strategy.md}} or similar). Reference ADR 0006 (the dev → main source-branch enforcement) and the squash-merge convention from {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}.
* If posture (a) is chosen: update {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} with explicit promotion-day operating instructions naming the back-merge step.
* If posture (b) or (c): implement the change, verify across one full Friday promotion cycle, retire the back-merge step.

Done when

* The git mechanics are documented in the ADR (or the issue body if no ADR is created), so future developers can read it once and understand why the divergence happens rather than treating it as a recurring mystery.
* An explicit posture decision is recorded.
* If implementation work is required, it lands and is verified across one promotion cycle.
* {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} is accurate with respect to the chosen posture.

Dependencies

* None hard. The investigation can run independently of any sprint work. Implementation work (if any) depends on no other ticket.

Out of scope

* Changing the squash-merge convention for {{feat/* → dev}}. The squash-merge for feature branches is settled; this ticket only investigates the {{dev → main}} step.
* Changing the {{Enforce dev → main}} source-branch restriction (ADR 0006). That stays.
* Replacing the entire branching model (e.g. moving to trunk-based development or a single-branch flow). Out of scope for this ticket.

Priority

Low. Operationally invisible - the manual back-merge workaround works and is cheap (one extra PR per week). Real architectural debt because it accumulates noise in {{dev}}'s history and means every promotion has an "extra step" that no one fully understands. Backlog candidate; could be fast-tracked if a future promotion exhibits a more serious divergence (e.g. blocked merge, lost commits) that the back-merge workaround doesn't cleanly resolve. Surfaced as a long-standing untracked operational quirk on May 1 2026 during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] close-out.
column53:
07/May/26 12:53 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Closed via PR #63, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

What shipped:

* Created {{docs/adr/0008-dev-to-main-merge-strategy.md}} (132 lines). Standard Status / Context / Decision / Consequences / References shape. Documents the recurring divergence pattern, root cause, three-posture investigation, and the rationale for selecting {{--no-ff}} over fast-forward-only.
* Modified {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} line 37: replaced the previous "squash-merge is the default for both stages" sentence with a two-sentence statement of the merge-type asymmetry ({{feat/* → dev}} squashes, {{dev → main}} uses {{--no-ff}} merge commits) and a pointer to ADR 0008 for rationale.

Decision recorded:

* {{feat/* → dev}}: squash-merge (unchanged from current practice).
* {{dev → main}}: {{--no-ff}} merge commit ("Create a merge commit" in the GitHub PR merge dropdown). The merge commit's body is the per-promotion audit record; {{git log --first-parent main}} yields a release-only timeline.
* The first {{--no-ff}} promotion absorbs the existing 43-vs-1 divergence as its two parents. No reconciliation PR was required before the switch.

Investigation evidence:

* Live-repo forensics: 2 back-merges since the branch model was established ({{f2469d7}} on 2026-04-24 with 3 conflict files; {{da5b7bf}} on 2026-05-01 with 23 conflict files). The 3→23 growth in one week was the load-bearing data point - the workaround's cost is non-constant and grows with the promotion's touch surface.
* 3-cycle sandbox reproduction confirmed the root cause: squashing at both stages keeps {{merge-base dev main}} pinned at the pre-first-promotion commit. Each cycle adds N commits unique to dev (the per-feature squashes) and 1 commit unique to main (the new release squash); divergence grows monotonically until reconciled.
* {{--ff-only}} was tested as an alternative and rejected: it requires a one-time reconciliation today (live {{main}} is not currently in dev's ancestry) and loses the release-PR audit trail because no commit is created on a fast-forward. {{--no-ff}} preserves the audit trail and needs zero setup.
* Investigation writeup: {{/tmp/AI-100-investigation.md}}. Sandbox is re-runnable via {{/tmp/AI-100-divergence/run-{squash,ffonly,noff}.sh}}.

Pre-implementation verification:

* {{.github/workflows/enforce-dev-to-main.yml}} inspected and confirmed merge-type-agnostic (pure PR-head-ref string check; runs no git commands; doesn't check out the repo). ADR 0006's source-branch enforcement is structurally unchanged.
* GHEC Pull Request settings confirmed to permit both merge commits and squash merging. No settings change required.

Validation:

* Tomorrow's Friday promotion (2026-05-08) is the first-cycle validation. The first {{--no-ff}} merge of {{dev → main}} will consume the existing divergence as its two parents, after which {{merge-base dev main}} will advance to dev's tip on every subsequent promotion.
* {{make hooks}} and {{make precommit}} clean. Reviewed and approved by Elijah.

Follow-up flagged but not filed:

* GHEC Pull Request "default commit message" setting may want a re-review after observing the first {{--no-ff}} merge's auto-generated subject. If the rendered subject is clean, no follow-up needed; if it's ugly, a 30-second settings flip from "Default message" to "Pull request title and description" is the fix. Decision deferred to post-promotion observation.

No other follow-ups. The back-merge workaround is permanently retired.

column1: Fix 0002_enable_pgvector downgrade: polisim_migrations cannot DROP EXTENSION vector
column27:
Surfaced during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] manual verification: a fresh-from-baseline downgrade chain ({{alembic downgrade base}} from {{0006_run_lineage}}) fails on {{0002_enable_pgvector}}'s downgrade with {{psycopg.errors.InsufficientPrivilege: must be owner of extension vector}}. The {{DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS vector}} statement at {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0002_enable_pgvector.py:38}} runs as {{polisim_migrations}}, but the {{vector}} extension is owned by the bootstrap superuser (likely created by the original {{0002}} upgrade running under different role context, or by initial DB provisioning).

The bug was masked through the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] cycles because every prior migration ticket only verified single-step round-trips (e.g. {{0005}} → {{0004}} → {{0005}}) which never reach {{0002}}'s downgrade. The fresh-baseline {{alembic downgrade base}} is the only verification path that exposes it, and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] was the first ticket where it was run from a populated state.

Operational impact: low for now. {{alembic downgrade base}} is not part of any normal workflow. CI doesn't run it. {{make reset-db}} (per [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s reset runbook) drops the database entirely rather than walking the downgrade chain, so resets are unaffected. The bug surfaces only when an operator or future agent manually runs {{alembic downgrade base}} from a populated state.

Architectural impact: real. The project's stated posture is that migrations are reversible, and one of the early migrations is not reversible from {{polisim_migrations}}. This contradicts the commit-message claims on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-12|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-12|smart-link] (when {{0002}} landed) and the broader reversibility guarantee in {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}'s "migrations reversible (or forward-fix protocol noted)" PR-checklist line.

Two reasonable fixes:

* (a) Grant {{polisim_migrations}} ownership of the {{vector}} extension in {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} and any higher-environment provisioning. Cleanest from a "consistent ownership posture" perspective. Requires a one-line {{ALTER EXTENSION vector OWNER TO polisim_migrations}} in {{init.sql}} plus a one-line addition to whatever provisioning script higher environments use (see [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link]'s hosting decision for that scope).
* (b) Soften the {{0002}} downgrade to {{op.execute("DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS vector CASCADE")}} and accept that the downgrade is best-effort. Less clean; doesn't actually fix the ownership mismatch; the {{IF EXISTS}} already covers the existence-not-found case but not the ownership-mismatch case.

Recommendation is (a). Aligns with ADR 0005's posture that {{polisim_migrations}} is the schema owner.

Deliverables

* {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}}: add {{ALTER EXTENSION vector OWNER TO polisim_migrations;}} after the role and extension creation. Confirm idempotency (the statement is safe to re-run).
* Consider whether the existing {{0002_enable_pgvector}} upgrade should set ownership explicitly: {{op.execute("CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector"); op.execute("ALTER EXTENSION vector OWNER TO polisim_migrations")}}. Decide based on whether {{init.sql}} runs before {{alembic upgrade head}} reliably across all environments.
* Update {{docs/adr/0005-tenancy-enforcement-mechanism.md}} if the §5 {{init.sql}} reference SQL block needs to reflect the new ownership statement (the live {{init.sql}} is the source of truth, but the ADR documents intent).
* Verify post-fix: from a fresh DB, {{alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade base && alembic upgrade head}} round-trips cleanly.

Done when

* {{alembic downgrade base}} from any populated state runs cleanly without {{InsufficientPrivilege}} errors.
* The fix is captured in {{init.sql}} (and optionally also in the {{0002}} upgrade for migrations-only provisioning paths).
* A test or runbook step confirms the fresh-baseline round-trip works.
* {{make reset-db}} still works (sanity check - the fix shouldn't affect the drop-database path).

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] merged (role provisioning is the foundation this fix sits on top of).
* Soft coupling to [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link] (hosting decision) - higher-environment provisioning will need the same fix; can be propagated to the AI-28 deliverable list when that ticket lands.

Out of scope

* Refactoring how extensions are managed in migrations more broadly (separate concern).
* Adding fresh-baseline downgrade to CI (separate ticket if desired; the current single-step round-trip CI coverage is intentional).
* Reviewing every other migration for ownership-bypass-via-DDL gotchas - none exist today; this is a {{vector}}-specific issue because extensions have ownership semantics that tables don't.

Priority

Low. Real reversibility gap but not blocking any sprint goal. Operationally invisible until someone runs {{alembic downgrade base}} from a populated state, which no current workflow does. Backlog candidate; could be fast-tracked if [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link] hosting work needs the higher-environment provisioning script.

=== UPDATE 2026-05-06 ===

Original recommendation invalid: Postgres has no {{ALTER EXTENSION ... OWNER TO}} form. Verified against {{\\h ALTER EXTENSION}} on PG16 - supported forms are {{UPDATE}}, {{SET SCHEMA}}, {{ADD}}, {{DROP}} only. Extension ownership is set at {{CREATE EXTENSION}} time and cannot be transferred. Compounding constraint: pgvector is non-trusted (no {{trusted = true}} in {{vector.control}}), so {{polisim_migrations}} could not be made the creator either, even with database-level {{CREATE}}.

Fix path changes from "ownership transfer in {{init.sql}}" to "downgrade becomes a no-op in the migration."

Revised deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0002_enable_pgvector.py}}: change {{downgrade()}} from {{op.execute("DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS vector")}} to a no-op with a comment block explaining: extension lifecycle is owned by {{init.sql}} / infra provisioning, not by the migration chain; Postgres has no {{ALTER EXTENSION ... OWNER TO}} form so {{polisim_migrations}} cannot self-heal the ownership mismatch; symmetric with the upgrade's documented "clean no-op" stance.
* No changes to {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}}. Current state is correct for upgrade.
* No changes to {{docs/adr/0005-tenancy-enforcement-mechanism.md}} §5. Extension provisioning was already out of §5's stated scope; this verification confirms it stays out.
* No changes to {{docs/runbooks/local-db-reset-for-rls.md}}. Original recommendation would have added a {{\\dx vector}} post-reset check; with the no-op fix the runbook stays correct as-is.

Revised done when

* {{alembic downgrade base}} from a populated state succeeds without {{InsufficientPrivilege}}. Extension stays present; that is intentional and consistent with the infra-owns-extensions posture.
* Round-trip {{alembic upgrade head}} after {{alembic downgrade base}} succeeds cleanly.
* {{make reset-db}} still works (sanity check unchanged).

Out of scope additions

* Squashing {{0002}} into {{0001}}'s baseline. Architecturally cleaner but rewrites alembic history; too big for [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|smart-link].

Files changed: 1 (the migration). Smaller than original spec.
column53:
07/May/26 6:38 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Completed via PR #57, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

Resolved the {{alembic downgrade base}} failure ({{psycopg.errors.InsufficientPrivilege: must be owner of extension vector}}) by making {{0002_enable_pgvector}}'s {{downgrade()}} a documented no-op. Extension lifecycle is owned by {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}}, not by the migration chain - {{upgrade()}} was already a documented "clean no-op" in this regime; {{downgrade()}} now matches.

Material finding from verification: the original ticket recommendation ({{ALTER EXTENSION vector OWNER TO polisim_migrations}} in {{init.sql}}) was invalid Postgres syntax. Verified against {{\\h ALTER EXTENSION}} on PG16 - no {{OWNER TO}} form exists; extension ownership is set at {{CREATE EXTENSION}} time and cannot be transferred. pgvector is also non-trusted (no {{trusted = true}} in {{vector.control}}), so {{polisim_migrations}} could not be made the creator either. Description was re-spec'd accordingly during execution.

Round-trip verified clean: {{make reset-db}} → {{alembic downgrade base}} → {{alembic upgrade head}} all exit 0. Extension remains present after {{alembic downgrade base}}, which is intentional and consistent with the infra-owns-extension-lifecycle posture. {{make reset-db}} remains the canonical way to drop the extension.

Files changed: {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0002_enable_pgvector.py}} (1 file, +25/-1).

{{init.sql}}, ADR 0005 §5, the local-db-reset runbook, and other migration files were deliberately left unchanged. The ADR 0005 §5 / {{init.sql}} drift remains tracked under [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s existing §5 sweep follow-up; this fix does not widen the gap.

Follow-up filed: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-107|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-107|smart-link] tracks the architecturally cleaner long-term answer - squashing {{0002}} into {{0001}}'s baseline so the migration chain stops modeling an extension it doesn't actually own. Out of scope for [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|smart-link] because it rewrites alembic history.

column1: Sweep three remaining forbidden-vocab leaks in PR/issue templates and ADR 0003
column27:
The April 30 project-file audit identified three forbidden-vocab leaks that [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|smart-link]'s synonym sweep explicitly excluded from scope. They reinfect every PR and every schema-change issue filed against the repo, and ADR 0003's {{run record}} reference contradicts the ADR 0004 vocabulary lock that all subsequent ADRs follow. Filing as a single combined sweep rather than three separate tickets - same rationale as [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|smart-link] and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link] used.

Deliverables

* {{.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md}}: replace {{PersonaTemplate}} with {{Persona}} per ADR 0004 decision 2.
* {{.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/schema_change.md}}: replace {{PersonaTemplate}} with {{Persona}} per ADR 0004 decision 2.
* {{docs/adr/0003-pgvector-in-postgres.md}}: replace {{run record}} (or {{run records}}) with {{SimulationRun record}} (or {{SimulationRun records}}) per ADR 0004 decision 4 and the carve-out for the capitalized phrase.
* {{rg -nw 'PersonaTemplate|RunRecord|run record|run records|Trace record|salience shift' .github/ docs/ README.md CONTRIBUTING.md CLAUDE.md}} returns either zero hits or hits only inside ADR 0004's "rejected alternatives" section after the sweep.

Done when

* The three files above grep-clean for the forbidden synonyms above.
* A repo-wide grep shows the only remaining hits are inside ADR 0004's rejected-alternatives section.
* Pre-commit clean.
* PR squash-merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|smart-link] merged (provides ADR 0004 as the citation target).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-74|smart-link] merged (the original non-canonical-doc sweep, which excluded these three by scope).

Out of scope

* Edits to {{docs/engineering-handoff.md}} or {{docs/engineering-distinctions.md}} (CEO vision artifacts; flagged informationally in [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|smart-link] but deliberately not edited).
* Any new ADR or new terminology decisions.
* Any code changes.
* A lint rule or pre-commit hook that catches forbidden vocabulary going forward - separate concern, file as its own ticket if desired.

Priority

Low. Pure documentation drift; no behavior change. Sprint 3 candidate.
column53:
04/May/26 7:51 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #52, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

Three single-line edits landed:

* {{.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md}} line 15: {{persona template snapshot}} → {{Persona snapshot}}; {{run-record field additions}} → {{SimulationRun field additions}}; {{run records}} → {{SimulationRun records}}.
* {{.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/schema_change.md}} line 26: {{persona template shape}} → {{Persona shape}}; {{run record shape}} → {{SimulationRun shape}}.
* {{docs/adr/0003-pgvector-in-postgres.md}} line 85: {{run record}} → {{SimulationRun record}} (ADR 0004 decision 4 carve-out form). Line 84's {{A run's embeddings}} preserved per the same decision's {{run}}-as-prose-shorthand rule.

Spec/reality gap noted in the commit body and PR scope: the ticket scope text named leaks using CamelCase example strings ({{PersonaTemplate}}, {{run record}}), but the actual surface forms in these files were lowercase prose ({{persona template}}, {{run record shape}}) and one hyphenated compound ({{run-record}}). Locked the broader reading - every forbidden-vocab surface form in the three deliverable files in scope - before drafting the diff. Coherent post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-98|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-98|smart-link] state required it.

Done-when verification:

* {{rg -nw 'PersonaTemplate|RunRecord|run record|run records|Trace record|salience shift' .github/ docs/ README.md CONTRIBUTING.md CLAUDE.md}} returns hits only in the three legitimate-mention locations: {{docs/adr/0004-canonical-terminology.md}}, {{docs/glossary.md}}, {{docs/canonical-data-model.md}} lines 6 and 8.
* {{make hooks}} and {{make precommit}} clean.
* Branch cut from {{dev}}, exactly 3 files staged, CI green on PR.

Deliberately untouched: {{docs/engineering-handoff.md}} and {{docs/engineering-distinctions.md}} (CEO vision artifacts, [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|smart-link] deliverable 6); {{docs/glossary.md}}, {{docs/canonical-data-model.md}}, {{docs/adr/0004-canonical-terminology.md}} (legitimate-mention carve-outs).

No follow-ups required from this ticket. The pre-commit hook / lint rule for forward-going forbidden-vocab enforcement remains out of scope as stated.

column1: Investigate pre-commit-on-commit vs make hooks parity — recurring auto-format catch
column27:
Both [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] hit the same pattern at commit time: CC ran {{make hooks}} (or equivalent) pre-staging and reported clean, then {{git commit}} triggered pre-commit's {{ruff-format}} to auto-fix files in place, aborting the commit. The remediation is mechanical ({{git add -u}} and re-commit), but the root cause is worth understanding because it means our local-clean signal is not a reliable proxy for commit-clean.

Possible causes worth investigating:

* {{make hooks}} invokes pre-commit with a different flag set ({{--all-files}}?) than the git pre-commit hook.
* The two ruff-format invocations target different file globs.
* The pre-commit hook runs against staged content; {{make hooks}} runs against working-tree content; if files are unstaged when {{make hooks}} runs but staged when commit runs, formatting can drift.
* Some quirk of the ruff-format config ({{exclude}}, {{extend-include}}) makes one invocation see files the other doesn't.

The fix is probably one of:

* Document in CONTRIBUTING.md or CLAUDE.md that the canonical pre-commit invocation before claiming clean is {{pre-commit run --files <staged-files>}} after {{git add}}, not {{make hooks}} against the unstaged tree.
* Or update {{make hooks}} to mimic the pre-commit hook's behavior exactly (run against {{--from-ref HEAD --to-ref :staged:}} or whatever flag captures the staged set).
* Or accept the pattern (one extra {{git add -u}} + re-commit per ticket) as cheap and document it as expected.

Deliverables

* Investigate and identify the actual divergence between {{make hooks}} and the pre-commit-on-commit hook.
* Either align them so a clean {{make hooks}} predicts a clean commit, OR document the divergence in CONTRIBUTING.md so the pattern is expected and the workaround ({{git add -u}} + re-commit) is named.
* If alignment is the chosen path, update the Makefile target.

Done when

* A clean {{make hooks}} (or the documented equivalent) predicts a clean commit on the next ticket touching Python files.
* CONTRIBUTING.md or CLAUDE.md names the canonical pre-commit invocation if alignment isn't possible.

Dependencies

* None. Depends only on existing pre-commit and Makefile config.

Out of scope

* Adding new pre-commit hooks.
* Replacing pre-commit with a different tool.
* Any code change outside Makefile, .pre-commit-config.yaml, and docs.

Priority

Medium. DX paper-cut; the workaround (one extra commit cycle) is cheap.
column53:
30/Apr/26 12:47 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #45, squash-merged to {{dev}}.

What landed

* New {{make precommit}} Makefile target running {{pre-commit run --hook-stage commit}}. Mirrors the installed git on-commit hook's file scoping (staged content of staged files), so a clean run predicts a clean commit by construction.
* 13-line comment block above the new target capturing both load-bearing pieces of the rationale: (a) why {{make hooks}} is misleading for new-file PRs (cites [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] vs. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] contrast), and (b) explicit anti-recommendation against switching to {{pre-commit run --files <…>}} (the C.3 silent-mutation anomaly).
* {{make hooks}} left untouched. Repo-wide drift check use case (pre-PR, post-rebase) preserved.
* CONTRIBUTING.md "Pre-commit hooks" subsection rewritten: replaces the one-line "Run all hooks manually" guidance with side-by-side two-target description plus the {{git add -u && git commit}} recovery flow framed as expected, not a bug.
* CLAUDE.md "What Claude Code should always do" bullet replaced: now names {{make precommit}} as canonical pre-commit-claim check and explicitly notes {{make hooks}} is not a reliable proxy for commit-cleanness on PRs that add new files.

Investigation findings

* Phase 1 read of {{Makefile}}, {{.pre-commit-config.yaml}}, {{.git/hooks/pre-commit}}, and {{pyproject.toml}} confirmed two visible divergences: file set (--all-files = tracked files; on-commit = staged set) and content source (--all-files = working-tree; on-commit = staged blobs).
* Phase 2 controlled experiments (Experiments A, B, C on a throwaway {{investigate/AI-97-precommit-parity-experiment}} branch) confirmed the file-set divergence directly. The dividing line is "before {{git add}}" vs "after {{git add}}", not "untracked vs tracked" as originally hypothesized - once a previously-untracked file is staged, {{--all-files}} does see it. The trap is the order of {{make hooks}} vs {{git add}}.
* Experiment C.3 surfaced an unanticipated upstream pre-commit behavior: {{pre-commit run --files <unstaged-path>}} silently mutates the file in place AND reports {{Passed}} with exit 0. No "Failed - files were modified by this hook" line. Reproduced twice. Read: pre-commit's "did this hook modify files?" detection compares against the staged blob, and an untracked file has no staged blob to compare against, so silent mutation goes undetected. This is a false-clean signal worth knowing about for any future "optimization" attempts. Captured inline in the Makefile comment block so future contributors don't re-discover it the painful way.

Decisions held during execution

* Chose {{pre-commit run --hook-stage commit}} over {{git diff --cached --name-only}} piped to {{--files}}. The {{--hook-stage commit}} form is pre-commit's documented invocation for "run as if this were the commit hook" and uses the same code path the actual hook does. The {{--files}} flavor would re-introduce the C.3 silent-mutation trap if anyone ever passes an unstaged path by mistake.
* Chose Approach B2 (split into two targets) over B1 (replace {{make hooks}}) or A (document-only). B1 would have changed the semantic meaning of an existing target - anyone with {{make hooks}} in muscle memory loses the repo-wide drift-check answer. B2 keeps {{make hooks}} semantically intact and adds a clearly-named new target.
* Some Approach A component included anyway: CONTRIBUTING.md and CLAUDE.md edits make the new convention discoverable. Makefile changes alone don't help anyone who doesn't read the Makefile.
* Inline Makefile comment chosen as the home for the C.3 anomaly note rather than CONTRIBUTING.md. The relevant decision (do not switch to --files) lives at the recipe site; the comment stays local to the code it protects.

Verification

* {{make precommit}} with nothing staged → all hooks Passed or Skipped, exit 0.
* {{make hooks}} → unchanged behavior, exit 0.
* Induced-failure verification: wrote a deliberately ill-formatted {{_ai97_fix_probe.py}}, staged it, ran {{make precommit}}, confirmed ruff-format Failed with the file reformatted. Cleaned up via {{git restore --staged}} + {{rm}}. Probe never committed.
* {{mypy --strict}} clean (38 source files; no Python source changed).
* 55 unit tests + 25 integration tests green.
* This PR's own diff did NOT trigger ruff-format auto-fix on commit - fourth datapoint matching the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] pattern. Self-consistent with the hypothesis we just shipped a fix for.
* CI green on PR.

Notable execution-time observations

* The fix PR itself was meta-recursive: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|smart-link] documents a commit-time auto-format pattern that historically afflicts new-file PRs, and the AI-97 fix PR (modifying three existing files only) did NOT hit that pattern. Empirically self-consistent with the hypothesis the PR is shipping the fix for.
* Phase 1 read time was minimal because investigation and fix were done in the same Claude Code session - file state was already in working memory. Same-session continuity saved a meaningful re-read step.
* The two-grep scope-confirmation pattern from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] (matching both {{from <module> import <name>}} and {{<name>(...)}} forms) was not directly reusable here, but the analogous induced-failure verification pattern from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link] (write deliberately broken probe, confirm fix catches it, clean up) was reused exactly. Worth treating as a documented verification-pattern repertoire going forward.

Patterns established

* {{make precommit}} as the canonical pre-commit-claim check, named in CLAUDE.md's "always do" section. CC's future working pattern uses this after staging.
* {{make hooks}} retained as the repo-wide drift check; CONTRIBUTING.md names the use cases (pre-PR, post-rebase).
* Inline Makefile comments as the home for "do not optimize this" rationale, with reference to the source investigation. Future contributors see the anti-pattern documented at the decision site.
* Induced-failure verification is now the standard for fix-shaped tickets where the verification surface is "did the fix actually fire?" - same shape as AI-31's CI integration-test induced-failure probe.

Unblocks / clears debt

* The [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] commit-time auto-fix surprise should not recur on future tickets that add new files. CC running {{make precommit}} after staging gives a deterministic predictor.
* Elijah-Monday-readiness: CONTRIBUTING.md is what he reads on day one. The two-target side-by-side comparison should make the right invocation obvious without him having to re-discover the divergence experimentally.

Out-of-scope items deliberately not addressed

* CI workflow files still use {{pre-commit run --all-files}}. Correct for CI's "is the whole repo clean?" question, distinct from the on-commit hook's "is this commit clean?" question.
* {{.pre-commit-config.yaml}}, {{pyproject.toml}}, {{.git/hooks/pre-commit}}: not touched. Hook configuration is not the divergence; flag combinations are.
* README.md, infra/README.md: not edited. Load-bearing surfaces are CONTRIBUTING.md (humans) and CLAUDE.md (CC sessions).
* The C.3 anomaly is upstream pre-commit behavior, not a PoliSim issue. Captured inline in the Makefile rather than filed as a separate "document upstream gotcha" ticket.

column1: Refactor: pick a single UUID column-type convention across models and migrations
column27:
AI-34's {{Organization}} model uses {{UUID(as_uuid=True)}} (imported from {{sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql}}); {{0003_create_organizations.py}} uses {{sa.Uuid()}} (the generic dialect-agnostic form). AI-55 introduced the same divergence in {{scenario.py}} vs {{0004_create_scenarios.py}}. AI-56's {{persona.py}} establishes {{sa.Uuid()}} as the new convention; this ticket sweeps the two existing model files into alignment. Both forms produce identical {{uuid}} Postgres columns; this is a code-aesthetics decision, not a correctness one.

The convention should be {{sa.Uuid()}} everywhere (matches AI-34's precedent and the {{UUIDPKMixin}} declaration in {{db/base.py}}). Pick before AI-56 / AI-57 introduce more UUID FK columns and the inconsistency multiplies.

Deliverables

* Update {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/scenario.py}} AND {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/organization.py}} to use {{sa.Uuid()}} instead of {{UUID(as_uuid=True)}}. Both model files currently use the dialect form. AI-56's persona.py establishes {{sa.Uuid()}} as the new convention.
* Remove the now-unused {{from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID}} import.
* Confirm {{mypy --strict src/}} clean and integration suite green — no DDL changes, no migration changes.
* Document the convention in a one-line comment or docstring addition somewhere appropriate (likely {{db/base.py}}'s module docstring or a {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} callout).

Done when

* {{rg "from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID" backend/src/}} returns no hits.
* All UUID columns across {{polisim/db/models/}} use {{sa.Uuid()}} or the {{UUIDPKMixin}}.
* No migration changes (DDL is identical).
* PR squash-merged to dev.

Dependencies

* AI-55 merged (the inconsistency this fixes).

Out of scope

* Renaming {{UUIDPKMixin}} or any column.
* Any change to migrations (DDL is identical between the two forms).

Priority

Medium. Cosmetic consistency fix; no behavior change. Worth doing before AI-57 / AI-76 / AI-80 add more UUID FK columns and the inconsistency proliferates.
column53:
30/Apr/26 12:05 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #44, squash-merged to {{dev}}.

What landed

* Swept {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/organization.py}} and {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/scenario.py}} from {{UUID(as_uuid=True)}} (postgresql dialect) to {{sa.Uuid()}} (generic SQLAlchemy core type). Matches the convention established in [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link]'s {{persona.py}} and the {{UUIDPKMixin}} declaration in {{db/base.py}}.
* Added {{import sqlalchemy as sa}} to both files in alphabetical position (above the {{from sqlalchemy import ...}} block, matching persona.py ordering). Both files were missing the import - Phase 1 surfaced this; ticket assumption that both already had it was wrong.
* Narrowed {{organization.py}}'s dialect import from {{from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import ARRAY, UUID}} to {{ARRAY}} only - {{ARRAY}} is still used by {{assigned_jurisdiction_ids}}. Deleted {{scenario.py}}'s dialect import line entirely (no other dialect types in use).
* Extended {{db/base.py}}'s module docstring with a one-line convention callout, placed as the closing sentence of the first paragraph: "Convention: {{sa.Uuid()}} is the canonical UUID column type across all models; {{UUIDPKMixin}} uses it for the primary key." This is now the authoritative reference for future model authors.
* Dropped a four-line stale forward-reference paragraph from {{persona.py}}'s docstring (lines 14–17 inclusive, including the leading blank line for visual balance). Documentation-rot cleanup: leaving "[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] will sweep {{organization.py}} and {{scenario.py}} to match" in past-tense context is documentation rot.
* Updated {{tests/unit/test_organization_model.py}} to assert {{sa.Uuid}} instead of the dialect {{UUID}} type. Same documentation-rot rationale as the persona.py drop: stale assertion is invalidated as a direct consequence of the convention sweep.

Verification

* {{rg "from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID" backend/src/ backend/tests/}} returns 0 hits.
* {{rg "UUID\\(as_uuid=True\\)" backend/src/ backend/tests/}} returns 0 hits.
* {{mypy --strict}} clean (36 source files).
* 55 unit tests + 25 integration tests green locally and on CI.
* {{make hooks}} clean - first ticket in four where ruff-format did NOT auto-fix on commit.
* Manual psql verification skipped by design. Zero DDL change; existing migrations ({{0003_create_organizations.py}} and {{0004_create_scenarios.py}}) already use {{sa.Uuid()}} migration-side, so the model-side swap produces identical column types. Manual schema sanity-checks are reserved for tickets that introduce or alter DDL.

Decisions held during execution

* Convention callout placement: chose {{db/base.py}} module docstring (closing sentence of first paragraph) over {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}. Local-context discoverability - {{db/base.py}} is where {{UUIDPKMixin}} lives, so future model authors find the convention adjacent to the mixin they're inheriting from.
* Persona.py paragraph drop: confirmed in-scope despite originally being out-of-scope. The forward-reference becomes inaccurate as a direct consequence of the sweep, so removing it is corrective, not scope-expansive. Same rationale extended to the test_organization_model.py assertion update.
* Test edit consolidated into the same PR, not split into a separate ticket. Strict-scope argument lost when we accepted the persona.py paragraph drop on the same documentation-rot rationale; consistent application of the rule means the test fix is also in-scope here.

Notable execution-time observations

* Phase 1 surprise: neither {{organization.py}} nor {{scenario.py}} imported {{sqlalchemy as sa}} - ticket assumption was wrong. CC's read-before-plan discipline caught this and reworked the file deltas to add the import.
* Phase 1 surprise: {{organization.py}}'s dialect import was a multi-name line ({{ARRAY, UUID}}); the original scope-confirmation grep {{from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import UUID}} missed it because of literal-substring mismatch. CC found it via the column-type grep instead. The two-grep verification approach is the right pattern - captured both forms and avoided the false-negative.
* Phase 1 surprise: {{persona.py}} docstring's stale forward-reference paragraph surfaced as a third hit in the column-type grep. Drop-the-paragraph decision (Option 1 in CC's elicitation) was the right call: cleaner persona.py, single source of truth in {{db/base.py}}, no stale-text-post-merge.
* Pre-commit ruff-format did not auto-fix on this commit. Notable [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|smart-link] datapoint: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] all hit the auto-fix pattern, but [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] broke the streak. The differentiating factor appears to be ticket shape - AI-54/55/56 created new files with longer import blocks and more typed columns; [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] only modified existing files with surgical edits. The auto-fix pattern is correlated with new-file creation, not "any commit hits it." Useful signal for [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|smart-link]'s investigation.

Patterns established

* Module docstring as the canonical home for codebase conventions, with cross-cutting rules placed at the most local mixin/helper that embodies them. The {{db/base.py}} placement makes the convention discoverable from the very mixin future model files inherit from.
* Documentation-rot cleanup is in-scope when stale text is a direct consequence of the change being made. Applied twice in this ticket: persona.py paragraph and test_organization_model.py assertion. Lower-friction than splitting into separate tickets.
* Two-grep scope-confirmation pattern (matched both {{from <module> import <name>}} and {{<name>(...)}} call forms) catches multi-name dialect imports and prevents false-negatives. Worth applying to future refactor-shaped tickets.
* Manual psql verification posture: required for tickets that introduce or alter DDL ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link]); not required for type-form refactors with zero DDL impact (this ticket). Worth codifying in CONTRIBUTING.md or CLAUDE.md as a reviewer guideline if it isn't already.

Unblocks / clears debt

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] (SimulationRun + AgentOutput) - the next model ticket - can declare new UUID FK columns as {{sa.Uuid()}} directly without re-litigating the choice or copying mixed conventions.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link] (composition layer models) and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|smart-link] (PromptTemplate) inherit the same clean slate.
* The codebase now has a single UUID column-type form across all five model files ({{organization.py}}, {{scenario.py}}, {{persona.py}}) and their three corresponding migrations ({{0003}}, {{0004}}, {{0005}}). One convention, one location, one source of truth.

Out-of-scope items deliberately not addressed

* Paragraph 2 of [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link]'s description still has stale wording ("Pick before [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] introduce more UUID FK columns" and "matches [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]'s precedent"). Neither was in the approved description-edit scope; left as cosmetic-only ticket-archaeology.

column1: Refactor: extract make_organization test fixture factory to dedupe Organization INSERT patterns
column27:
Three near-identical Organization INSERT patterns now exist across two test files: {{seed_organization}} and {{seed_two_organizations}} in {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}}, plus {{seed_inactive_organization}} local to {{backend/tests/integration/test_tenancy_resolution.py}}. Each constructs an Organization with the same set of fields, wraps it in a similar try/insert/yield/delete block, and varies only on a few axes (slug prefix, {{is_active}} flag, count).

[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] (Scenario), [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] (Persona), and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] (SimulationRun + AgentOutput) all need Organization fixtures of various shapes for their tenant-scoped test setups. Without dedup, this pattern multiplies. The cheap fix is a factory.

Deliverables

* New helper at {{backend/tests/integration/_factories.py}} (or equivalent path that signals "test-only utility"): {{async def make_organization(*, session_or_engine, is_active: bool = True, slug_prefix: str = "seed", **overrides) -> Organization}}. Inserts and returns. Caller is responsible for cleanup, OR provide a context-manager variant.
* Refactor {{seed_organization}}, {{seed_two_organizations}}, and {{seed_inactive_organization}} to call the factory. Behavior preserved exactly.
* One small unit test on the factory itself (it constructs the right shape; defaults work; overrides override).
* Document in the file's module docstring: this is the canonical way to construct Organization rows in integration tests; one-off inline INSERTs are a smell.

Done when

* All three existing fixtures use the factory.
* No behavior change in any existing test.
* Integration suite green.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.

Dependencies

* None hard. Lands cleanest after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link].

Out of scope

* Factories for other models. Scenario, Persona, and SimulationRun get their own factories in their respective tickets or a follow-up.
* Switching to a fixtures library (factory-boy, etc.). Hand-rolled is sufficient pre-MVP.
* Any production code changes.

Priority

Low. Test-quality refactor; no behavior or coverage change. Worth doing before the pattern proliferates further in [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link]+.
column53:
04/May/26 10:01 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #54 ({{refactor(tests): extract _make_organization factory and consolidate seed_inactive_organization (AI-95)}}), squash-merged to {{dev}}. CI green.

The duplicate Organization construct + insert pattern across three integration-test fixtures is now consolidated. {{_make_organization}} lives in {{conftest.py}} alongside {{_milspouse_persona}} / {{_urban_progressive_persona}} (matching repo precedent - no {{_factories.py}} module created for a single helper). Factory takes an injected {{AsyncEngine}} and owns construct + insert; calling fixtures keep engine lifecycle and row cleanup. Correct-by-construction id/slug pairing via {{setdefault}} after {{**overrides}} merge - no silent skew possible if a future caller passes {{id=}} or {{slug=}} directly.

Three files modified:

* {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}}: adds {{_make_organization}}, refactors {{seed_organization}} and {{seed_two_organizations}} to delegate, adds the relocated {{seed_inactive_organization}}, updates module docstring.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_tenancy_resolution.py}}: removes the local {{seed_inactive_organization}} fixture (32 lines) and prunes 7 now-unused imports. *Scope expansion* - the relocation was a near-miss for shared extraction independent of the factory work; folded inline rather than spawning a follow-up since the factory work made it cheap. Same precedent as [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-93|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-93|smart-link]'s {{health.py}}.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_organization_migration.py}}: appends a four-assertion smoke test (defaults applied, slug prefix used, overrides override, per-call slug uniqueness). Uses relative {{from .conftest import _make_organization}} per src-layout convention; absolute imports from {{tests/}} are fragile.

Behavior change explicitly surfaced in the commit body: {{seed_inactive_organization}} no longer sets {{name="Inactive Seed Org"}} - uses the factory's default {{"Seed Org"}}. Cosmetic only; consumers assert on slug + status, never on name. A future {{git log -S 'Inactive Seed Org'}} lands on the commit explanation.

Atomicity note for {{seed_two_organizations}}: the original's single-{{engine.begin()}} block gave atomic rollback for free; the refactored two-factory-call shape loses that property if the second call raises after the first commits. Compensated with defensive None-guarded cleanup. Multi-row factory variant rejected as scope creep.

Verification:

* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean across 39 source files. {{backend/tests/}} is under {{--strict}} per the repo's mypy config; {{**overrides: Any}} carries a justifying comment per CLAUDE.md's no-bare-Any rule.
* Unit tests: 103 passed.
* Integration tests: 40 passed (one more than baseline - the new factory smoke test). All three refactored fixtures produced byte-for-byte equivalent outcomes; every consumer test passed without modification.
* {{make hooks}} and {{make precommit}} clean.
* Manual smoke: {{cd infra && docker compose --profile apps up -d}} brought DB + API + web cleanly; {{/readyz}} returned 200 with {{{"status":"ok"}}}.

The manual-smoke step surfaced an operator-friction finding outside [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-95|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-95|smart-link]'s scope: the {{apps}} compose profile is not exposed via a Makefile target - operators have to read {{make help}}'s parenthetical and reconstruct the {{docker compose --profile apps up -d}} invocation manually. Filed as [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-103|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-103|smart-link] (parent epic [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-1|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-1|smart-link]) for follow-up.

No follow-ups needed from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-95|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-95|smart-link] itself. Factories for Scenario / Persona / SimulationRun / AgentOutput remain explicitly out of scope per the ticket; file separately if/when proliferation justifies it.

column1: DX: make psql target plus operator doc for postgresql+psycopg:// scheme and host-side env override for integration tests
column27:
Two recurring stumbles, same root cause: the dual-environment problem where {{infra/.env}} carries values appropriate for the in-compose api container ({{db:5432}}) but operators and integration tests on the host need {{localhost:5432}}.

Stumble 1: psql cannot parse the SQLAlchemy driver suffix in {{DATABASE_URL}} ({{postgresql+psycopg://...}}) and silently falls back to socket defaults, producing a confusing {{/var/run/postgresql/.s.PGSQL.5432}} error. Hit during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] manual verification.

Stumble 2: sourcing {{infra/.env}} directly into a host shell ({{set -a; source infra/.env; set +a}}) injects {{db}} as the DB hostname into the host shell, breaking {{uv run pytest -m integration}} from the host. Hit during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] post-commit verification.

Both have an in-band fix (a {{make}} target that does the right thing) and an out-of-band fix (an operator-doc paragraph in {{infra/README.md}}). Ship both.

Deliverables

* New Makefile target {{make psql}} that connects to the local compose Postgres as {{polisim_app}} with the right host/port/db. Strips the driver suffix or constructs the connection from individual settings - must work from a clean host shell with no env pollution.
* Optional: {{make psql-migrations}} for the {{polisim_migrations}} role, used for owner-bypass operations.
* {{infra/README.md}} update: a "Common operator tasks" section covering connecting psql to the local stack, running integration tests from the host with the correct {{DATABASE_URL}}, and what NOT to do (do not source {{infra/.env}} directly into a host shell).
* {{backend/.env.test}} or equivalent host-side env file with {{localhost:5432}} URLs, OR a {{make test-integration}} target that injects the override before invoking pytest. Pick whichever fits existing test-running conventions; document in the same {{infra/README.md}} section.

Done when

* {{make psql}} from a fresh host shell connects successfully to the local Postgres without manual URL surgery.
* {{make test-integration}} (or equivalent) runs the integration suite from the host without polluting the shell environment.
* {{infra/README.md}} documents both, plus the gotcha that caused the original confusion.
* Pre-commit clean.

Dependencies

* None hard. Light coupling to [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link] (Postgres CI service container) - if [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link] introduces a different test-env pattern, this ticket's {{make test-integration}} should align.

Out of scope

* Rebuilding the env-management story end-to-end (separate Settings instances per env, dotenv hierarchy, etc.). Tactical fix only.
* Any change to the in-container behavior.
* Any change to how CI runs tests (CI has its own env injection per workflow YAML).

Priority

Low. DX paper-cut, recurring but small.
column53:
01/May/26 10:43 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #48, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

Three new {{make}} targets, a new operator-doc section in {{infra/README.md}}, and a side-fix to the stale {{web}} paragraph.

What landed:

* {{Makefile}}: {{make psql}} and {{make psql-migrations}} shell into the local {{db}} container as {{polisim_app}} and {{polisim_migrations}} respectively via {{docker compose exec}}. No host-side URL needed.
* {{Makefile}}: {{make test-integration}} runs the integration suite with host-side {{DATABASE_URL}} / {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} injected inline. Mirrors the env block in {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}}'s {{backend-integration-test}} job.
* {{infra/README.md}}: new {{## Common operator tasks}} section between {{## Files}} and {{## Resetting after AI-88 role provisioning}}. Documents the three new targets and names the don't-source-{{infra/.env}}-into-your-host-shell gotcha explicitly.
* {{infra/README.md}} side-fix: stale {{web}} paragraph at L19-20 replaced with current post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link] service description (matches {{infra/docker-compose.yml:40-57}}).

Approach choice for {{psql}} targets: {{docker compose exec db psql ...}} rather than rewriting {{DATABASE_URL}} in shell. {{Settings()}} exposes only composed {{PostgresDsn}} strings, not components; container-internal connection sidesteps both the driver-suffix and {{db}}→{{localhost}} rewrite issues. Pattern is consistent with {{docs/runbooks/local-db-reset-for-rls.md}}.

Approach choice for {{test-integration}}: inline env injection at the recipe site, not a {{.env.test}} file. Mirrors CI's pattern and avoids a third {{.env}} to keep in sync.

Verification: {{make psql}} and {{make psql-migrations}} connected and {{\\q}}'d cleanly from a fresh shell. {{make test-integration}} → 39/39 passed in 1.99s. {{make precommit}} clean on two consecutive runs.

Out-of-scope follow-up filed: {{infra/README.md:29}} ("Full-stack command intentionally omitted until [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link] lands {{web}}") is also stale post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link] but was deliberately left for a separate doc-sweep ticket so this PR stayed single-concern. Ticket draft prepared during planning; will file as a follow-up below.

column1: Refactor: move get_unscoped_session to db/session.py to break deps↔resolver import cycle
column27:
[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] introduced a circular-import workaround in {{backend/src/polisim/api/deps.py}}: a module-level late import of {{get_current_org}} from {{polisim.tenancy.resolver}}, with a {{# noqa: E402}} and an inline-justification comment, placed after {{get_unscoped_session}} is defined. This works because {{tenancy.resolver}} imports {{get_unscoped_session}} from {{deps.py}}, and {{deps.py}} imports {{get_current_org}} from {{tenancy.resolver}} - two arrows in opposite directions through the same module.

The clean structural fix is moving {{get_unscoped_session}} out of {{deps.py}} and into {{db/session.py}}, where session-layer plumbing already lives (alongside {{get_sessionmaker}}, {{init_engine}}, {{dispose_engine}}). Then {{tenancy.resolver}} imports from {{polisim.db.session}}, {{deps.py}} imports from {{polisim.tenancy.resolver}}, no cycle, no {{noqa}}, no inline comment. {{deps.py}} becomes purely the request-scoped dependency layer it was always meant to be - no session lifecycle code.

Deliverables

* Move {{get_unscoped_session}} from {{backend/src/polisim/api/deps.py}} to {{backend/src/polisim/db/session.py}}. Preserve the docstring (the ADR 0005 Decision 13 carve-out language is load-bearing).
* Update {{polisim.tenancy.resolver}} to import {{get_unscoped_session}} from {{polisim.db.session}} instead of {{polisim.api.deps}}.
* Remove the late import, {{# noqa: E402}}, and inline comment block from {{deps.py}}.
* Update {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}} import path for {{get_unscoped_session}}.
* Update module docstrings: {{db/session.py}} to mention the new resident; {{api/deps.py}} to drop the {{get_unscoped_session}} mention; {{tenancy/__init__.py}} already references {{polisim.api.deps.get_unscoped_session}} - update to the new path.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean. Integration suite green. Pre-commit clean.

Done when

* {{get_unscoped_session}} is defined in {{polisim.db.session}}, not {{polisim.api.deps}}.
* No late imports anywhere in the resolver/deps chain.
* All existing tests pass without modification beyond import-path updates.
* PR squash-merged to dev.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] merged (the cycle this fixes).

Out of scope

* Renaming {{get_unscoped_session}} or {{get_tenant_session}}. Names are stable.
* Any change to the dependencies' bodies or semantics.
* Refactoring how the resolver consumes the session.
* Auth model changes.

Priority

Low. Pure structural cleanup; no behavior change. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link]'s late-import workaround is correct and tested but reads as architectural debt. Backlog candidate.
column53:
04/May/26 8:43 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #53, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

The [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] circular-import workaround is gone. {{get_unscoped_session}} now lives in {{polisim.db.session}} alongside {{init_engine}}, {{dispose_engine}}, and {{get_sessionmaker}} - the existing home of session-layer plumbing. {{polisim.api.deps}} imports {{get_current_org}} as a normal top-of-file import; no late import, no {{# noqa: E402}}, no five-line inline-justification comment.

Six files modified (four from the ticket deliverables + scope expansion):

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/session.py}}: gains the function (placed after {{get_sessionmaker}}, the logical home). Module docstring rewritten to reflect split residency.
* {{backend/src/polisim/api/deps.py}}: loses the function definition, the late-import block, and the inline-justification comment. Module docstring rewritten - drops the carve-out paragraph (the function's own docstring is the canonical statement of the carve-out now) and cross-references {{polisim.db.session.get_unscoped_session}} as a single source of truth.
* {{backend/src/polisim/tenancy/resolver.py}}: import-path swap.
* {{backend/src/polisim/tenancy/__init__.py}}: docstring module-path string updated.
* {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}}: import-path swap.
* {{backend/src/polisim/api/routes/health.py}}: import-path swap. Scope expansion - the ticket deliverables list four files; {{health.py}} is a fifth call site (the {{/readyz}} consumer the ticket scope text acknowledges but the deliverables list omits). Folded inline rather than spawning a follow-up; import-collection breakage on {{health.py}} is part of the same refactor's blast radius, not separable work. Same pattern as [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-98|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-98|smart-link]'s surface-form spec/reality gap - coherent post-refactor state required it.

Verification:

* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean across 39 source files. Load-bearing static check for this refactor.
* Unit tests: 103 passed.
* Integration tests: 39 passed, including {{test_db_readyz}} (the live {{broken_db_override}} exerciser at {{conftest.py:289-311}}) and {{test_tenancy_resolution}}. Confirms function-identity preservation across the module move - {{Depends(get_unscoped_session)}} annotations and {{dependency_overrides}} keying resolve correctly without further change. The callable-identity claim holds.
* {{make hooks}} and {{make precommit}} clean. ADR 0005 Decision 13 docstring rationale carried forward verbatim.

Operational note: PR #53 was initially opened against {{main}} by mistake. Caught and rebased to {{dev}} via the GitHub UI base-edit, which triggered an "Expected - Waiting for status to be reported" stuck-check state because base changes don't fire a {{synchronize}} event. Resolved with an empty {{ci:}} commit to retrigger checks. Worth flagging in case [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|smart-link]'s {{enforce-dev-to-main}} workflow should have fired on the original {{main}}-targeted PR and didn't - see follow-up below.

Follow-up needed: file a ticket investigating whether [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|smart-link]'s {{enforce-dev-to-main}} workflow fires correctly on PRs initially opened against {{main}} from non-{{dev}} source branches. If it didn't fire on PR #53's initial main-targeted state, the safety net [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-67|smart-link] was supposed to provide isn't actually catching the case it was built for. Will draft separately.

column1: Sweep: ADR 0005 textual inconsistencies surfaced during AI-91
column27:
Four textual inconsistencies in ADR 0005 surfaced during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link] diff review. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link] was strictly scoped to §3 and §5 SQL/narrative corrections; these four items live outside that scope but in the same ADR. Filing them as a single combined sweep rather than four separate tickets - same rationale as AI-91's combined §3/§5 framing.

None of these are load-bearing. The live code in {{policy_helpers.py}} and {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} ships the correct behavior; this ticket aligns the remaining ADR text with what's already in the code.

Item 1 - apply_tenant_only_policy docstring (file lines ~369–371)

The docstring describes the unwrapped cast form ({{current_setting('app.current_org_id', true)::uuid}} without {{NULLIF}}). Inconsistent with the SQL block immediately following it, which [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link] corrected to use {{NULLIF}}.

Action: update the docstring to describe the {{NULLIF}}-wrapped cast form, matching the SQL block.

Item 2 - Decision 5 narrative

Decision 5 asserts: "the two-argument form returns NULL when the GUC is unset." This is incomplete - Postgres returns {{''}} (empty string) instead of NULL once the GUC has been referenced earlier in the session, even after a transaction-scoped value reverts. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] surfaced this empirically; [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link]'s §3 narrative addition documents the post-revert nuance, but Decision 5 itself still claims clean NULL semantics.

Action: amend Decision 5 to acknowledge the {{''}} post-revert case and reference §3's {{NULLIF}} wrap as the deterministic fix. Roughly two sentences added.

Item 3 - Decision 10 narrative

Decision 10 has inline-backtick policy descriptions using the unwrapped cast form ({{current_setting('app.current_org_id', true)::uuid}} without {{NULLIF}}). Same drift as Item 1, different location.

Action: update the inline-backtick descriptions to use the {{NULLIF}}-wrapped form.

Item 4 - In-SQL comment in §5's init.sql spec block

The in-SQL comment describing {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES}} reads: "polisim_migrations owns schema objects. ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES ensures future tables created in the public schema are owned by polisim_migrations (the migration runner) rather than the bootstrap superuser." This misdescribes the statement - {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES}} sets default GRANTS on tables, not ownership. Ownership comes from the role that runs {{CREATE TABLE}}. The live {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} (lines 22–27, 38–46) has a more precise comment block.

Action: rewrite the in-SQL comment to accurately describe what {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES}} does - specifically, that it grants DML on tables created later by {{polisim_migrations}} to {{polisim_app}}. Pull the precise wording from the live {{init.sql}} block.

Deliverables

* Four edits to {{docs/adr/0005-tenancy-enforcement-mechanism.md}} per the actions above.
* No code, test, or migration changes.
* Pre-commit clean.

Out of scope

* Any code changes (live SQL already correct via [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]).
* Any other ADR sections beyond the four locations above.
* Generalizing the {{NULLIF}} documentation pattern to other ADRs (no other ADR currently documents {{current_setting}} casts).

Priority

Low. Pure documentation drift; not blocking any sprint goal. Backlog candidate.
column53:
01/May/26 9:38 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #47, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

* Decision 10: NULLIF-wrap applied to both {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy}} inline-backtick policy descriptions.
* §5 reference SQL {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES}} comment: replaced with near-verbatim wording from {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} lines 44-46. The ADR no longer misdescribes ADP as setting ownership.

No code, test, or migration changes. No follow-up tickets.

column1: Sweep: correct ADR 0005 §3 (NULLIF) and §5 (FOR ROLE) reference SQL
column27:
Two corrections to ADR 0005 reference implementation surfaced during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] execution. Both are textual fixes to the ADR; neither is load-bearing because the live code in {{policy_helpers.py}} and {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} already implements the corrected forms. This ticket aligns the ADR text with what [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] actually shipped.

Correction 1 - §3 reference SQL (NULLIF wrap on current_setting casts)

ADR 0005 Decision 5 specifies clean zero-row fail-closed semantics when {{app.current_org_id}} is unset. The §3 reference SQL casts {{current_setting('app.current_org_id', true)}} directly to {{uuid}}:

{noformat}organization_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id', true)::uuid{noformat}

This errors with {{invalid input syntax for type uuid: ""}} when Postgres returns {{''}} instead of NULL - which it does once the GUC has been referenced in a session, even after a transaction-scoped value reverts. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] wraps each cast in {{NULLIF}}:

{noformat}organization_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_org_id', true), '')::uuid{noformat}

The {{<> '' AND}} form was tried first and rejected because Postgres does not guarantee short-circuit evaluation of AND in RLS predicates. {{NULLIF}} is the deterministic fix.

Action: update §3 reference SQL - both {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy}} - to wrap every {{current_setting(...)::uuid}} in {{NULLIF(..., '')}}. Add a paragraph to the §3 narrative explaining the issue and why {{NULLIF}} rather than AND-guard. Approximately 10 SQL fragment edits across the two helper bodies.

Correction 2 - §5 reference SQL (FOR ROLE clause on ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES)

§5 reference SQL omits the {{FOR ROLE}} clause:

{noformat}ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public
    GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO polisim_app;{noformat}

Without {{FOR ROLE polisim_migrations}}, default privileges only attach to tables created by the role running the statement (the bootstrap superuser running {{init.sql}}), not to tables created later by {{polisim_migrations}}. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] implements:

{noformat}ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE polisim_migrations
    IN SCHEMA public
    GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO polisim_app;{noformat}

Action: update §5 reference SQL to include the {{FOR ROLE polisim_migrations}} clause. One-line edit.

Deliverables

* ADR 0005 §3 reference SQL updated for both helpers; narrative paragraph added explaining the {{NULLIF}} rationale.
* ADR 0005 §5 reference SQL updated with {{FOR ROLE}} clause.
* A short note in ADR 0005 references [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] as the implementing ticket where these corrections originated.
* No code changes required - {{policy_helpers.py}} and {{init.sql}} already ship the corrected forms.

Out of scope

* Any changes to live SQL or migrations (corrected forms already shipped in [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]).
* Generalizing the {{NULLIF}} pattern to other GUC casts (no other GUC casts exist yet; [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] onward will inherit the corrected helper).
* Changes to ADR 0005 §Verification - the test cases there describe the intended behavior, which the corrected SQL now actually delivers.

Priority

Low. Documentation drift fix; not blocking any sprint goal. Backlog candidate.
column53:
29/Apr/26 1:49 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Closed via PR #38, squash and merged to {{dev}}.

What shipped

* §3 narrative paragraph added explaining the {{NULLIF}} rationale and the rejected {{<> '' AND}} guard form (Postgres does not guarantee short-circuit evaluation of AND in RLS predicates).

No code, test, or migration changes - this PR aligned ADR text with what [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] already shipped.
