column1: Role provisioning, two-URL config, and RLS helper module
column27:
Lands the deferred ADR 0005 plumbing that [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] deliberately scoped out. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] (Organization model) shipped with its migration running as the bootstrap superuser; AI-55 (Scenario, the first tenant-scoped child table) needs RLS policies applied via the helper module, which in turn requires the {{polisim_app}} / {{polisim_migrations}} role split, two-URL config, and {{env.py}} rewrite. This ticket is the gate before [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link].

Per ADR 0005:

* Decision 11: two roles, {{polisim_app}} (DML grants only, not table owner, no {{BYPASSRLS}}) and {{polisim_migrations}} (table owner, no {{BYPASSRLS}}). Bypass for migrations is via table ownership, not role attribute.
* Decision 12: {{FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY}} deliberately NOT enabled.
* Decision 14: two database URLs. {{DATABASE_URL}} (asyncpg, app) and {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} (psycopg, Alembic). {{env.py}} reads {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} directly rather than the {{+asyncpg → +psycopg}} replacement pattern from ADR 0002 §2.
* Reference implementation §5 and §6 of ADR 0005 give the exact SQL and Python content.

The helper module ships in this ticket as the named functions defined in ADR 0005 reference implementation §3 - {{apply_tenant_only_policy(table_name)}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy(table_name)}}. First concrete caller is [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] (tenant-only) and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|smart-link] / future JurisdictionFeature ticket (tenant-or-system).

Deliverables

* {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}}: role-creation script per ADR 0005 §5. Mounted into the Postgres container's {{/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/}} so roles are provisioned on first DB init.
* {{infra/docker-compose.yml}}: mount the init.sql script; add {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} to environment surface.
* {{infra/.env.example}}: add {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS=postgresql+psycopg://polisim_migrations:<localdevonly-password>@db:5432/polisim_dev}}. Update {{DATABASE_URL}} to use {{polisim_app}} instead of the bootstrap superuser.
* {{backend/src/polisim/config.py}}: add {{database_url_migrations: PostgresDsn}} field to {{Settings}}.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/env.py}}: rewrite per ADR 0005 §6 to read {{database_url_migrations}} directly. Remove the {{+asyncpg → +psycopg}} replacement.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/policy_helpers.py}} (or {{polisim.db.tenancy.policy_helpers}}): module containing {{apply_tenant_only_policy(table_name: str)}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy(table_name: str)}} per ADR 0005 §3. Module docstring per project convention: what it IS for / what it is NOT for. Functions wrap {{op.execute(...)}} calls; safe to call only from inside Alembic migrations.
* Local-dev reset path: document the reset procedure in {{infra/README.md}} (or equivalent). Existing devs need to recreate their local DB to pick up the role split. Procedure is {{make reset-db}} style - drop volume, recreate, run init.sql, run migrations. The reset itself is a host-side privileged-ish step, so reference a runbook at {{docs/runbooks/local-db-reset-for-rls.md}} rather than embedding commands in this ticket.
* {{docs/runbooks/local-db-reset-for-rls.md}}: the actual host-side commands for the reset, version-controlled per the project's no-shell-commands-in-tickets rule.
* Tests: integration test that confirms {{polisim_app}} cannot SELECT from a future-tenant-scoped table without GUC set (uses {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} against a throwaway test table to verify the helper works end-to-end). Tests live alongside the helper module.
* Tests: integration test that confirms {{polisim_migrations}} can DDL via owner-bypass without {{BYPASSRLS}}.

Done when

* {{make dev}} brings up the stack with both roles provisioned via init.sql.
* {{make migrate}} runs as {{polisim_migrations}} against {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}}.
* Application connection ({{get_db}} / future {{get_tenant_session}}) connects as {{polisim_app}} against {{DATABASE_URL}}.
* {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy}} exist as importable functions, each verified by integration test against a throwaway tenant-scoped test table.
* {{infra/README.md}} documents the reset procedure; runbook exists and references the right env-var and volume names.
* All existing tests still pass ({{pytest tests/}}, {{pytest -m integration tests/integration/}}).
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.
* Pre-commit clean.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] merged (Organization model exists; migration pattern established).
* ADR 0005 (already merged, defines the spec).

Out of scope

* Wiring the helper into a real RLS-protected table - that's [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] (Scenario) and onward.
* The auth-aware {{get_current_org()}} resolver and the consolidated tenant-aware FastAPI dependency - that's [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link].
* Lint rule for "missing organization_id filter" - separate future ticket per ADR 0005 Future Work.
* Admin role with {{BYPASSRLS}} for cross-tenant analytics - future ADR per ADR 0005 Future Work.
* Higher-environment role provisioning (dev / staging / prod) - depends on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link] hosting decision; environment-specific scripts owned by the environment owner.
* Renaming {{get_db}} → {{get_tenant_session}} - [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link]'s call.

Priority
High. Hard gate on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link].
column53:
29/Apr/26 1:30 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Closed via PR #37, squash-merged to {{dev}}.

What shipped

* Two-role provisioning via committed {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}}, mounted into the db container at {{/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql}}. {{polisim_app}} gets DML grants only via {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE polisim_migrations}}; {{polisim_migrations}} is the table owner. Neither role has {{BYPASSRLS}}. {{init.sql}} also pre-creates the pgvector extension as the bootstrap superuser, making migration 0002's {{CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector}} a clean no-op when re-run as {{polisim_migrations}}.
* Two-URL config per ADR 0005 Decision 14: {{DATABASE_URL}} (asyncpg, app, {{polisim_app}}) and {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} (psycopg, migrations, {{polisim_migrations}}) are now distinct fields on Settings. {{database_url_migrations}} is required (no default) - strict reading of Decision 14. Both {{backend/.env.example}} and {{infra/.env.example}} updated; runbook tells operators to update both their local {{.env}} files before {{make reset-db}}.
* {{env.py}} rewritten to read {{database_url_migrations}} from Settings (not {{os.environ}} directly per ADR 0005 §6's sketch). Matches existing {{get_settings()}} pattern; preserves the post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] {{from polisim.db import models}} registration line.
* {{polisim.db.policy_helpers}} ships {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} and {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy}}. Flat module under {{polisim.db}} (not {{polisim.tenancy}} - the post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link] tenancy package docstring explicitly excludes policy DDL helpers). No concrete caller in this ticket; AI-55 (Scenario) is the first.
* {{make reset-db}} Makefile target plus {{docs/runbooks/local-db-reset-for-rls.md}} cover the volume-roll required for engineers with pre-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] {{pgdata}}. Runbook explicitly calls out that skipping the {{.env}} updates leaves the API connecting as the old superuser and silently defeats Decision 11.
* Integration test {{test_policy_helpers.py}} with 5 assertions: cross-tenant SELECT returns zero rows, cross-tenant INSERT rejected, migration-role owner-bypass works, missing-GUC fails closed (fresh session), missing-GUC fails closed after prior {{set_config}} (post-revert path). Two-engine fixture pattern in {{conftest.py}} - {{migrations_engine}} for setup/teardown DDL via Alembic {{Operations.context}}, separate asyncpg-on-{{database_url}} path for the {{polisim_app}} verification side. {{SET ROLE}} rejected: granting cross-role membership would broaden the bypass surface against Decision 11's intent.
* CI placeholder {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} added to Backend - Tests env block. Settings validates at import time; without the placeholder the whole job goes red. CI does not connect to Postgres (still no service container, deferred to [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link]).

Conventions established for future tickets

* Helper module location for policy DDL: {{polisim.db.policy_helpers}} (flat). Reused by [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] onward; promote to a {{polisim.db.tenancy}} subpackage only if a second policy-DDL helper appears.
* Two-engine fixture pattern for any test that needs to verify behavior across the role split. {{migrations_engine}} for DDL setup/teardown, app_engine (asyncpg, {{polisim_app}}) for the runtime-side assertion. Codified in {{conftest.py}}.
* Throwaway-table naming for RLS verification tests: {{_test_<scope>_<purpose>}} prefix to make psql-side cleanup obvious if a test crashes mid-run. {{_test_rls_throwaway}} used for [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s helper test; [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] will follow the pattern.
* Reset-DB workflow: {{make reset-db}} is the canonical entry point for "roll my local volume forward after a tenancy/role-related migration." Documented in the runbook, points devs to update both {{infra/.env}} and {{backend/.env}} before running. Same pattern will apply for any future ticket that changes role provisioning.
* PR body convention for ADR deviations: numbered list under a "Deliberate deviations from ADR 0005" heading, with the deviation reasoning inline so a reviewer diffing against the ADR text understands the intentional divergence. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] ships four; future tickets that touch ADR-specified SQL or role config should follow the same convention.

Deliberate deviations from ADR 0005

# §5 - {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES FOR ROLE polisim_migrations}} clause. ADR sketch omits the {{FOR ROLE}} clause; without it, default privileges only attach to tables created by the role running {{init.sql}} (the bootstrap superuser), not to tables created later by {{polisim_migrations}}. {{init.sql}} includes the corrected form.
# §5 - single-statement LOGIN role creation. Local dev needs LOGIN from the start; ADR's NOLOGIN form is for the abstract role definition, with login attribute layered per environment.
# §6 - {{env.py}} reads from Settings, not {{os.environ}} directly. Matches the existing {{env.py}} pattern; Settings is the single source of truth.
# §3 - {{NULLIF}} wrap on {{current_setting(...)::uuid}} casts. The original cast errors with {{invalid input syntax for type uuid: ""}} when Postgres returns {{''}} instead of NULL (which it does once the GUC has been referenced in a session, even after a transaction-scoped value reverts). {{NULLIF}} coerces both never-set and post-revert cases to NULL so the cast becomes {{NULL::uuid}} (legal, fail-closes cleanly per Decision 5).

Real defect caught and fixed pre-commit

The {{NULLIF}} fix wasn't planned. The original implementation followed ADR 0005 §3 reference SQL verbatim. Manual verification at the psql prompt surfaced that {{current_setting('app.current_org_id', true)}} returns {{''}} (empty string) rather than NULL after a transaction-scoped GUC reverts, causing the uuid cast to error instead of fail-closing cleanly. First fix attempt added a {{<> '' AND}} guard before the cast - rejected after CC reproduced empirically that Postgres does not guarantee short-circuit evaluation of AND in RLS predicates; the planner could still execute the cast against the empty string. {{NULLIF}} is the correct shape: it canonicalizes {{''}} to NULL before any cast, no boolean short-circuit involved, behavior deterministic. Fixed in {{policy_helpers.py}} and the regression test {{test_missing_guc_fails_closed_after_prior_set_config}} locks it in.

The defect would have shipped if we'd skipped manual verification. Worth recording as the canonical example of why manual psql sanity-check stays mandatory for any tenant-scoped table ticket from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] onward - automated tests passed against this version, but the actual Postgres behavior differed from the ADR's specified semantics in a subtle way that only surfaced when manually swapping connections and queries at the prompt.

Deferred items / follow-ups

* ADR 0005 sweep ticket ( [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-91|smart-link]): correct §3 reference SQL to use {{NULLIF(current_setting(...), '')::uuid}} and §5 reference SQL to include {{FOR ROLE polisim_migrations}} on the {{ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES}} clause. Combined sweep - both are §-level corrections to the same ADR. Low priority.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link] Postgres CI service container: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s role provisioning is now in place, so AI-31's CI job can use the role split when it lands. AI-31 description already accounts for this; no action needed here.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] ({{get_current_org}} / consolidated tenant-aware FastAPI dependency): the consumer of the runtime GUC contract validated by [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s manual verification. Critical path next ticket if continuing on the tenancy track.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] (Scenario, first concrete RLS-protected table): first caller of {{apply_tenant_only_policy}}. Manual psql verification of RLS behavior is non-optional for this ticket - see "Real defect caught" section above.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-89|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-89|smart-link] ({{updated_at}} posture decision): unrelated to [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]'s work but lives in the same general area; revisit alongside [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] or whenever the next {{TimestampMixin}} table lands.

column1: (CI hygiene): Bump Node 20 actions to Node 24 majors, investigate transient cache-service 400s
column27:
PR #36 ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]) surfaced 5 CI warnings worth addressing before they become failures. (1) Node.js 20 deprecation across all three CI jobs. The following actions are running on Node 20 and will be force-migrated to Node 24 on June 2nd, 2026, then removed entirely from runners on September 16th, 2026: * {{Backend — Tests}}: {{actions/checkout@v4}}, {{astral-sh/setup-uv@v4}} * {{Pre-commit hooks}}: {{actions/cache@v4}}, {{actions/checkout@v4}}, {{actions/setup-python@v5}} * {{Frontend — Lint & Build}}: {{actions/checkout@v4}}, {{actions/setup-node@v4}} Most of these have published Node 24–compatible majors already; bumping them is a single coordinated PR across the three workflow files. (2) {{actions/cache@v4}} reported transient failures during PR #36's {{Backend — Tests}} run: "Failed to save:

Our services aren't available right now

" and "Failed to restore: Cache service responded with 400". The job was green (cache failures are non-fatal - slower job, no test impact), but two cache errors in one job is worth attention. Most likely a transient GitHub Actions outage; if it recurs across the next 2–3 PRs, investigate cache-key collisions or the 10 GB per-repo eviction limit. Deliverables * Audit {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}} and any other workflow files; bump every action pin to a Node 24–compatible major. * Verify each bumped action's release notes for breaking changes (most are pure runtime bumps, but {{actions/cache}} has had API changes between majors). * Run the bumped pipeline on a probe PR; confirm all jobs green and no new warnings. * Watch the next 2–3 PRs for {{cache service responded with 400}} recurrence. If it recurs, file a separate investigation ticket; if it stops, close as transient. Done when * All workflow files use Node 24–compatible action versions. * A probe PR run shows zero deprecation warnings. * The cache 400s either don't recur or have a separate investigation ticket filed. Dependencies * None. Independent CI hygiene work. Out of scope * Adding mypy --strict to CI (separate follow-up ticket). * Adding Postgres service container + integration test job (separate follow-up ticket). * Investigating individual workflow performance regressions unrelated to action versions. Priority Medium. Sprint 3 candidate.
column53:
07/May/26 6:43 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Completed via PR #59, squashed and merged to {{dev}}. CI green on bumped workflow.

Bumped every Node 20-based action pin in {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}} to its current Node 24-compatible major ahead of GitHub's June 2 brownouts and September 16 removal:

* {{actions/checkout}} v4 → v6 (×4 occurrences; Node 24 since v5.0.0)
* {{astral-sh/setup-uv}} v4 → v7 (×2; Node 24 since v7.0.0)
* {{actions/setup-python}} v5 → v6 (×1; Node 24 since v6.0.0)
* {{actions/setup-node}} v4 → v6 (×1; Node 24 since v5.0.0)

Pure pin updates. No workflow restructuring, no input-syntax adaptations required.

Material findings during execution:

* {{astral-sh/setup-uv}} stops at {{@v7}}, not {{@v8}}. v8.0.0 discontinued publishing the floating {{@v8}} / {{@v8.0}} major tags; pinning at {{@v7}} preserves our floating-major-tag convention. Worth knowing if anyone tries to "finish the job" by bumping further.
* The original ticket listed {{actions/cache@v4}} as part of the bump set, but the workflows don't pin {{actions/cache}} directly. Caching is delegated via {{setup-uv}}'s {{enable-cache: true}} and {{setup-node}}'s {{cache: "npm"}}. The only {{actions/cache@v4}} reference in our toolchain is internal to {{pre-commit/action@v3.0.1}}, a composite action we don't fork. The bump list is four actions, not five.

CI run on PR #59 came back green across all four jobs ({{Backend — Tests}}, {{Backend — Integration tests}}, {{Pre-commit hooks}}, {{Frontend — Lint & Build}}) with zero Node 20 deprecation warnings — confirming end-to-end that the bumped workflow runs correctly on real GHA runners.

Files changed: {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}} (1 file, 8 line replacements).

Cache 400 watch from the original ticket: deferred per the ticket's own protocol — watch the next 2-3 PRs after this one for {{cache service responded with 400}} recurrence. If the errors return, file a separate investigation ticket targeting cache-key collisions or the 10 GB per-repo eviction limit. If they don't, the original cache failures resolve as transient with no further action needed. Not filing a follow-up preemptively.

Out of scope (per ticket): mypy --strict job and Postgres integration-test job remain untouched as separate follow-ups.

column1: Cloud deployment for Celeste-team access
column27:
The April 23 scope requires the system to be reachable by Celeste's team. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link]is the upstream decision; this ticket implements whichever hosting choice gets made.

Deliverables

* Backend deployment: container image build (per [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-21|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-21|smart-link]) deployed to the chosen cloud. Compose-stack-equivalent or k8s-equivalent in the target environment.
* Frontend deployment: Next.js standalone build deployed (Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, or self-hosted per [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link]).
* Postgres: managed service (RDS, Cloud SQL, equivalent) with {{pgvector}}. Encrypted at rest. Backups configured.
* Secrets: cloud provider's secrets manager. Database URLs, API keys, JWT signing key all stored there — not in env files.
* DNS + TLS: real domain (e.g. {{polisim-staging.kenaidefense.com}}), TLS via Let's Encrypt or provider equivalent.
* Basic observability: structured logs aggregated (CloudWatch / equivalent), error tracking (Sentry or equivalent), basic uptime monitoring.
* IaC: Terraform or equivalent, in {{infra/}}. Manual clicks once, then reproducible.
* Deployment runbook in {{docs/deployment.md}} (or extending [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-49|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-49|smart-link]).

Done when

* Celeste-team-accessible URL works end-to-end.
* Deployment is reproducible ({{terraform apply}} on a fresh account → same setup).
* Secrets in secrets manager, not env files or repo.
* Basic monitoring dashboards exist.
* Runbook is real (someone other than Kevin can deploy from it).

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-28|smart-link] merged (hosting decision).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-21|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-21|smart-link] merged (image build / push to registry).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link] merged (CI integration tests).

Out of scope

* Multi-region / multi-cloud HA.
* Auto-scaling beyond expected Celeste-team load.
* Production hardening (WAF, DDoS, advanced rate limiting).
* DR beyond DB backups.
* Compliance certification (SOC 2, FedRAMP).

Priority

High. Sprint 4 candidate.

column1: Frontend pages: scenario entry, progress, formatted report, runs index
column27:
The April 23 scope listed three frontend deliverables beyond Sprint 3's JSON-dump viewer: scenario entry, progress polling, and formatted report view. Plus a runs index page so users can find prior runs without pasting URLs. This ticket covers all four at coarse granularity; will likely split into 2–3 tickets at Sprint 4 planning.

Deliverables

* {{frontend/src/app/scenarios/new/page.tsx}}: form with textarea for scenario {{content}}, plus structured fields per canonical model §5.1 ({{scenario_type}} dropdown, {{messenger}}, {{implementation_notes}}). Submit POSTs to {{/api/v1/simulate}}, redirects to {{/runs/[id]/progress}}.
* {{frontend/src/app/runs/[id]/progress/page.tsx}}: polls {{/api/v1/runs/{id}/status}} every 2s. Shows status indicator (queued → running → complete) and basic progress signals (agent count). Redirects to {{/runs/[id]}} on complete; error UI on failed.
* {{frontend/src/app/runs/[id]/page.tsx}}: re-skin from Sprint 3's JSON dumps to a formatted report. Per persona: stance with intensity bar, top objections list, best counterframes, likely public language, evaluator scores. Citations rendered inline if sourced-citations work has landed.
* {{frontend/src/app/runs/page.tsx}}: index of recent runs scoped to user's org. Each row: scenario name, run timestamp, status, persona count. Click → {{/runs/[id]}}.
* Loading and error states throughout.

Done when

* A user can: log in → create scenario → wait for run → see formatted report → find it again from the index.
* TypeScript strict, lint clean.
* PR merged.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-79|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-79|smart-link] merged (Sprint 3 viewer is the starting point).
* Real auth merged (login flow).
* Async run execution + status endpoint merged.

Out of scope

* File upload UI on the scenario form. Pending Jeremiah.
* PDF/HTML export buttons.
* Run comparison views.
* Charts / advanced visualization.
* Mobile-responsive polish. Desktop-first for MVP.

Priority

High. Sprint 4 candidate.

column1: Frontend /runs/[id] read-only run viewer (JSON dumps)
column27:
Frontend page at {{/runs/[id]}} that fetches a {{SimulationRun}} by URL param and renders the response as monospace JSON dumps. Deliberately ugly — Sprint 3 demo to Jeremiah is architecture-proof, not capability-proof. Run IDs are pasted into the URL during the demo; no {{/runs}} index page in Sprint 3 (Sprint 4 work). The page fetches {{/api/runs/{id}}} (proxied to backend {{/api/v1/runs/{run_id}}}) and renders three sections: run metadata, composed stack info (active personas), agent outputs (one per persona, full JSON). No interactivity beyond loading. Deliverables * {{frontend/src/app/runs/[id]/page.tsx}}: client component fetching the run by ID. Three labeled sections, each rendering {{
{JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}
}} for its slice. * {{frontend/src/components/RunMetadata.tsx}}, {{RunStack.tsx}}, {{RunAgentOutputs.tsx}}: small components per section. * {{frontend/next.config.ts}}: rewrite for {{/api/runs/:id}} → {{${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/runs/:id}}. * Loading state: simple "Loading run…" text. * Error states: 404 → "Run not found." 401 → "Not authorized." 5xx → "Server error." * No layout polish, no styling beyond default Tailwind. Monospace fonts on JSON dumps. column1: Add cross-reference from ADR 0002 to ADR 0005's §2 deviation
column27:
ADR 0005 (Decision 14) explicitly concedes a deviation from ADR 0002 §2's "developers maintain one env var, not two" line, splitting the database URL into {{DATABASE_URL}} (app, asyncpg) and {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} (migrations, psycopg) so the app can run as {{polisim_app}} while Alembic runs as {{polisim_migrations}}. ADR 0005 cites the deviation in its Decision and Negative consequences sections, but ADR 0002 has no return-pointer - a future reader of ADR 0002 reading "one env var, not two" would not know that decision was superseded for tenancy reasons.

This ticket lands the missing return-pointer as a small footnote-style addition to ADR 0002 §2.

Deliverables

* Append a short note to {{docs/adr/0002-sqlalchemy2-alembic.md}} §2 (the "one env var" decision) cross-referencing ADR 0005's Decision 14 as the conceded deviation. Phrasing roughly: "Superseded for tenant-scoped tables by ADR 0005 Decision 14 - RLS requires the app and Alembic to run as different Postgres roles, which forces a second URL ({{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}}) for the migration role. The 'one env var' principle still applies to all non-tenancy-driven URL decisions."
* The note lives at the bottom of §2 as a callout, footnote, or trailing paragraph - not a rewrite of the original decision text. ADR 0002's original wording stays intact; the cross-reference is additive.

Done when

* ADR 0002 §2 has a return-pointer to ADR 0005 Decision 14.
* A reader landing on ADR 0002 §2 sees the deviation acknowledged without having to discover ADR 0005 independently.
* Diff is exactly one file, ~3-5 added lines, no other changes.

Dependencies

* Depends on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link] having merged so ADR 0005 Decision 14 exists as a citation target. (Already merged.)

Out of scope

* Any rewrite of ADR 0002's original §2 decision. The original "one env var" principle stands for non-tenancy URL decisions.
* Any other ADR cross-referencing or audit-trail work.

Priority

Low. Mechanical follow-up; no behavior change. Closes a known loose end from the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link]. Should land soon to prevent doc drift.
column53:
28/Apr/26 11:28 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Merged via PR #34, merged to {{dev}}.

Adds the missing return-pointer in {{docs/adr/0002-sqlalchemy2-alembic.md}} §2 acknowledging supersession by ADR 0005 Decision 14 for the tenancy-driven case. Original §2 decision text preserved byte-identical; the cross-reference is appended as a new continuation paragraph within the same numbered list item.

The added paragraph names {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}}, the {{polisim_app}} / {{polisim_migrations}} role separation, and the load-bearing reason (RLS bypassed by table owners requires the migration role to own tables while the app role is subject to RLS). The "one env var, not two" principle remains in force for all non-tenancy-driven URL choices.

Closes a known loose end identified during the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link].

column1: Decide tenancy enforcement mechanism and codify in ADR 0005
column27:
Canonical data model v0.2 §8 establishes that every tenant-scoped table carries {{organization_id}} and that no query on tenant-scoped data executes without that filter, enforced at the application layer rather than by convention. The mechanism is not yet picked. Four candidates are on the table: Postgres Row-Level Security (RLS), a {{TenantScopedMixin}} + repository-pattern combo, a SQLAlchemy session-scoped event-listener filter, and a mypy lint guard. They are not mutually exclusive - a defense-context production setup may layer two - but a primary mechanism needs to be chosen so [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]'s {{Organization}} model and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link]'s {{get_current_org()}} work against a known target. The wrong choice is recoverable but expensive once five tenant-scoped migrations have shipped against it. The decision needs to weigh: security posture for a defense-context multi-tenant product, testability, dev velocity, performance, and onboarding cost for an engineer joining 5/4.

Deliverables

* ADR at {{docs/adr/0005-tenancy-enforcement-mechanism.md}} evaluating all four options against named criteria (security, testability, performance, DX, onboarding), selecting one (or a layered combination), and explicitly recording why each rejected option was rejected.
* A reference implementation sketch in the ADR - pseudocode or a sample model class - concrete enough that [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]'s task prompt can reference it directly without reinterpretation.
* Test-coverage expectations: which behaviors [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] must demonstrate to prove enforcement is actually working (e.g., a query in tenant A's session returns zero rows for tenant B's data; raw SQL bypasses are documented as a known gap or covered by a second layer).
* Identification of any new dependencies the chosen mechanism introduces (libraries, Postgres extensions, session-management patterns) so they land in {{pyproject.toml}} and the compose stack as part of [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link], not as a surprise.

Done when

* ADR 0005 is merged to {{dev}} via PR using the standard template.
* The reference implementation sketch is detailed enough that drafting the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] task prompt requires no further design decisions.
* Rejected alternatives section is concrete with named tradeoffs.

Dependencies

* Cleaner if [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-72|smart-link] lands first so the ADR uses {{Organization}} consistently. Not a hard block.
* Blocks [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] and every subsequent tenant-scoped table ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|smart-link] evidence packs).

Out of scope

* Implementing the chosen mechanism - that's part of [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] (model + base class) and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] (request-scoped resolution).
* Choice of authentication or identity provider; {{Organization.id}} resolution at request-time is upstream of this decision.
* RBAC within an organization. Single-tier "user belongs to one org" is the assumed model for now.
* Cross-organization data sharing patterns. v0.2 §9.1 already settled that client-provided data is exclusive to its org.
* Deciding licensed-data retention policy. Deferred per v0.2 §9.1 until first licensed dataset arrives.

Priority
High. Gates [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] DDL. Has to merge before any model with {{organization_id}} is written.

Unblocks [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-66|smart-link].

column1: Lock canonical terminology and codify in ADR 0004
column27:
The canonical data model and engineering reports use several terms with overlapping or ambiguous meanings. {{Segment}} (engineering-distinctions) vs {{IdeologyBase}} / {{StateLens}} / {{MicroOverlay}} (canonical data model v0.2) - is "Segment" a synonym, an umbrella, or deprecated? {{Persona}} vs {{PersonaTemplate}} - same object or distinct? {{Agent}} - runtime concept only, or also a code-level class name? {{Run}} vs {{RunRecord}} vs {{SimulationRun}} - pick one. These terms appear in every downstream ticket description, every model class name, every prompt variable, and every doc page. Locking them now is cheap; retrofitting after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] ships is grep archaeology. The design review (v0.2 §9.1) already resolved scope-related terminology by implication; this ticket preserves those decisions rather than relitigating them.

Deliverables

* ADR at {{docs/adr/0004-canonical-terminology.md}} with one decision section per ambiguous pair, rationale, and rejected alternatives.
* Updated {{docs/canonical-data-model.md}} (bumped to v0.3) reflecting the locked terms wherever they appear.
* Sweep of {{docs/}} for any remaining drift; either fix in this PR or open a follow-up ticket per file (don't leave drift as TODO comments).
* New {{docs/glossary.md}} if one does not exist, or an addition to an existing glossary, capturing the locked terms in one searchable place.

Done when

* ADR 0004 is merged to {{dev}} via PR using the standard template.
* {{docs/canonical-data-model.md}} v0.3 is consistent with the ADR end-to-end.
* A grep for the rejected synonyms across {{docs/}} returns either zero hits or hits only inside ADR 0004's "rejected alternatives" section.
* Subsequent ticket descriptions ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link]) can reference these terms without redefinition.

Dependencies

* None as input. Blocks [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link]. Ideally lands before [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link] so ADR 0005 uses the locked {{Organization}} term consistently, but the two ADRs can also be drafted in parallel.

Out of scope

* Tenancy enforcement mechanism ( [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-73|smart-link] / ADR 0005).
* Renaming any existing code; there are no schema-level references to {{Segment}} / {{IdeologyBase}} in the repo yet.
* Defining {{Scenario}} schema beyond what canonical-data-model v0.2 already specifies.
* Naming conventions for prompts, evaluators, or runtime classes that don't appear in canonical-data-model.md (those will lock when the relevant tickets are scoped).

Priority
High. Gates the entire Product Foundation epic and is cheap to fix today versus after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] ships.

column1: Vendor improve-codebase-architecture skill, consolidate skills storage, document in CLAUDE.md
column27:
The mattpocock skills toolkit is partially vendored under {{.claude/skills/}} from PR #24 (design-an-interface, grill-me, tdd, to-issues, ubiquitous-language). A sixth skill, improve-codebase-architecture, was installed locally via {{npx skills@latest add}} but the installer's delivery model has changed since PR #24 - it now writes the actual files to {{.agents/skills/<name>/}} and drops a symlink at {{.claude/skills/<name>/}}, leaving an untracked {{.agents/}} directory and a {{skills-lock.json}} manifest at repo root.

If we commit only the symlink, a fresh checkout breaks. If we commit {{.agents/}} as well, we end up with two storage locations for the same content and a partial lockfile that only tracks the latest install.

CLAUDE.md also has no top-level catalog of installed skills. The only existing reference is a procedural rule for {{to-issues}} at CLAUDE.md:128-131. New engineers have no map of what's available.

Deliverables

* Replace the {{.claude/skills/improve-codebase-architecture}} symlink with a real folder containing copies of the four files from {{.agents/skills/improve-codebase-architecture/}} (SKILL.md, DEEPENING.md, INTERFACE-DESIGN.md, LANGUAGE.md). Matches the storage pattern of the previously vendored five.
* Delete {{.agents/}} and {{skills-lock.json}}. The lockfile is incomplete (tracks only the latest install) and {{.agents/}} is redundant once files are copied.
* Add {{.agents/}} and {{skills-lock.json}} to {{.gitignore}} under the existing "# Claude Code" block so future {{npx skills}} runs do not pollute the working tree.
* Add an "Installed skills" section to CLAUDE.md placed after "## Stack (deferred)" and before "## Repository layout". Catalogs all six vendored skills with one-line descriptions. Documents the manual consolidation procedure for future installs. Cross-references the existing {{to-issues}} procedural rule at CLAUDE.md:128-131 rather than duplicating it.

Done when

* {{.claude/skills/improve-codebase-architecture/}} is a real folder containing four {{.md}} files; no symlink remains at that path.
* {{.agents/}} and {{skills-lock.json}} are absent from the working tree and present in {{.gitignore}}.
* {{git check-ignore .agents/ skills-lock.json}} confirms both are now ignored.
* {{CLAUDE.md}} has an "Installed skills" section cataloging all six skills ({{grill-me}}, {{to-issues}}, {{design-an-interface}}, {{ubiquitous-language}}, {{improve-codebase-architecture}}, {{tdd}}) with the manual consolidation procedure documented.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* None. Foundation epic substantially complete; this is governance/tooling work that runs on top of it.

Out of scope

* Re-vendoring or modifying the five skills already on {{dev}} from PR #24.
* Modifying the existing {{to-issues}} procedural rule at CLAUDE.md:128-131; the new section cross-references it.
* Automating the install-and-consolidate flow (e.g., a Makefile target or a wrapper script). The manual procedure is fine while the toolkit is small; revisit if it becomes a friction point.
* Pinning skill versions or implementing a real lockfile. Defer until skill updates start landing in PRs and a versioning need emerges.

Priority

Medium

column1: Bump main/dev ruleset required approvals to 1 for team phase
column27:
[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link] configured the {{main}} and {{dev}} Rulesets with required approvals = 0 per {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}'s staged-onboarding pattern. This matches solo-phase development (solo engineer self-merging after CI passes). Team phase starts 2026-05-04 with Elijah's onboarding.

This ticket flips both Rulesets from required approvals = 0 to required approvals = 1 on that date. Pure GHEC admin task - no repo code changes.

Deliverables

* Update {{main}} Ruleset: required approvals 0 → 1.
* Update {{dev}} Ruleset: required approvals 0 → 1.
* Verify with an intentional empty-commit PR that self-merge is now blocked on the author and requires a second approver.
* Update {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} if any wording needs to reflect the team-phase configuration.

Done when

* Both Rulesets enforce 1 required approval.
* Self-merge attempt blocked on the author, verified by intentional test PR (can be closed without merging).
* {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} accurate with respect to current enforcement.

Dependencies

* Elijah has GHEC access and has merged his first PR (onboarding ticket, tracked separately).

Out of scope

* Adding Elijah to {{CODEOWNERS}} - separate onboarding ticket.
* Bumping required approvals above 1 - reconsider only if team grows past 3 engineers.
* Changing any other Ruleset configuration (status checks, conversation resolution, force push, etc.) - those are correct as-is from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link].

Priority:

Medium

column1: Enforce dev -> main source-branch restriction via required status check
column27:
[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link] migrated branch protection from classic rules to Rulesets on the assumption that Rulesets support restricting the source branch of a pull request. That assumption was incorrect - no rule in either classic branch protection or Rulesets on GHEC supports source-branch restriction today. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|smart-link]'s incident gap (feature branch accidentally PR'd directly to {{main}} instead of routed through {{dev}}) therefore remains open after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link]'s migration ships.

The standard workaround is a GitHub Actions workflow that validates the source branch on {{pull_request}} events targeting {{main}} and fails the check if the source is not {{dev}}. The workflow is marked as a required status check in the {{main}} Ruleset, so a non-{{dev}} PR to {{main}} cannot merge even though it can be opened.

This ticket implements that workaround, closes the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|smart-link] gap, and restores the behavior that [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link] was originally meant to enforce.

Deliverables

* New workflow at {{.github/workflows/enforce-dev-to-main.yml}}
** Triggers: {{pull_request}} events with base = {{main}} (opened, synchronize, reopened, edited - the {{edited}} trigger matters because a PR author can change the base branch after opening)
** Job: single job that reads {{github.head_ref}}
** Exit 0 if {{head_ref == 'dev'}}
** Exit 1 otherwise, with a workflow-step log line explaining why
** Optionally: post a PR comment on failure via {{gh}} CLI explaining the dev → main policy (nice-to-have, not required for done)
** Uses {{ubuntu-latest}}, no external actions beyond {{actions/checkout}} if needed - keep dependency surface minimal
* Mark the new check as a required status check in the {{main}} Ruleset (Settings → Rules → Rulesets → {{main branch protection}} → add to the existing required-checks list alongside {{backend-test}} and pre-commit)
* Update {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} to reflect the real enforcement mechanism and reinforce the dev → main merge path as both policy and mechanically enforced
* Verify: open an intentional test PR from a non-{{dev}} branch targeting {{main}}; confirm the check fails with a clear "source must be dev" message; confirm the PR cannot merge. Close/delete the test PR and branch after verification.
* Verify: open a {{dev}} → {{main}} PR; confirm the check passes. Close without merging (the real dev → main promotion is its own separate event).

Done when

* A PR to {{main}} from a non-{{dev}} branch cannot merge due to an attributable "source must be dev" status check failure.
* A PR from {{dev}} to {{main}} passes the check.
* {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} describes the enforcement mechanism honestly.
* Workflow file is linted and passes pre-commit.
* Successful and failing test PRs are referenced in the close comment with their PR numbers for audit.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link] merged (Rulesets in place to attach the required check to).

Out of scope

* Source-branch policy for future branch patterns ({{release/*}}, {{hotfix/*}}, etc.) - handled when those patterns exist.
* Bypass list for emergency direct-to-{{main}} operations - separate concern, deferred until a documented need emerges.
* Broader CI/CD workflow restructuring - this ticket adds a single focused workflow, not a refactor of existing CI.
* TypeScript/frontend equivalent - not applicable; this is a branch policy enforcement, not a language-specific check.

Priority:

High.
column53:
28/Apr/26 12:10 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Merged via PR #35, merged to {{dev}}.

 Repo-side changes complete:

* New workflow at {{.github/workflows/enforce-dev-to-main.yml}} validates that PRs to {{main}} originate from {{dev}}. Pure bash on {{${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}}} via env block; {{permissions: contents: read}} only; triggers {{[opened, synchronize, reopened, edited]}} (the {{edited}} trigger closes the base-branch-retarget bypass path).
* New ADR 0006 ({{docs/adr/0006-source-branch-enforcement-via-ci.md}}) records why the workflow exists despite Rulesets being the obvious place - the Rulesets gap, the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|smart-link] incident gap, the {{pull_request_target}} carve-out for single-org EMU, and the revisit criterion if GHEC ever adds platform-level source-branch restriction.
* CONTRIBUTING.md L33 and README.md L148 rewritten to name {{Enforce dev → main}} as the enforcement mechanism instead of stating the convention bare.

The required-check identifier {{Enforce dev → main}} (single U+2192 rightwards arrow, single spaces) is consistent across all five surfaces: workflow top-level {{name:}}, job {{name:}}, CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md, ADR 0006.

h2. Manual follow-ups

# Ruleset config (today or tomorrow): Settings → Rules → Rulesets → main → "Require status checks to pass" → add {{Enforce dev → main}} (typed manually; GHEC accepts unseen check names). Once added, the requirement is dormant until the workflow exists on {{main}}, at which point it activates.
# Verification PRs (post-Friday-promotion): Deferred until the workflow is carried to {{main}} via the next {{dev → main}} weekly promotion (Friday 5/1). The workflow only fires on PRs whose base is {{main}} and only when the file exists on {{main}}; this PR targets {{dev}}, so the check cannot run on its own PR, and the immediate promotion PR will be evaluated against a {{main}} tip that does not yet have the file. Two verification PRs needed after promotion lands:
#* Negative case: PR from a non-{{dev}} branch → check must fail with the "source must be dev" log line → PR unmergeable.
#* Positive case: PR from {{dev}} → check must pass → PR mergeable. Close without merging unless it's a real promotion.

h2. Closes

[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-10|smart-link] (incident gap that [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-27|smart-link] was meant to enforce but Rulesets cannot).

column1: Elijah onboarding: local stack running and first exploratory PR
column27:
Elijah's first ticket is deliberately structured as a single onboarding item rather than a pile of sub-tickets for "install Docker," "clone the repo," "configure git," etc. The goal of the first day or two is to get a working local PoliSim stack on his machine, get his bearings in the codebase, and ship a small, low-risk PR so he exits week one with the muscle memory of the PR flow rather than the anxiety of "I haven't committed anything yet."

The small PR target is deliberate. It is not meaningful product work; it is a forcing function for touching every part of the workflow - branching, pre-commit, PR template, CI, squash-merge - with something where scope is obviously not a source of risk.

Deliverables

* WSL2 + Ubuntu 24.04, pyenv + Python 3.12 (via {{uv}}'s managed Python), nvm + Node 20, Docker Desktop with WSL integration, VS Code with Remote-WSL extension. Setup walkthrough is split across {{README.md}} (§Prerequisites, §Clone and set up, §Run the local stack, §Database and migrations, §Troubleshooting), {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} (§Local setup, §Pre-commit hooks - run {{make install-hooks}} after first clone), and {{infra/README.md}} (§Common operator tasks). EMU-specific Git auth (SSH SSO authorization, PAT host, commit-author email) is covered in the day-1 cheat sheet.
* Repo cloned from GHEC, push access verified via an empty commit on a throwaway branch that is deleted after.
* {{make migrate}} applies all migrations through the current head without errors.
* Full test suite runs green: {{uv run pytest}} (unit, from {{backend/}}) and {{make test-integration}} (integration, with stack up - the Make target injects the localhost-based URLs the integration tests need; running {{pytest -m integration}} directly will fail unless backend/.env is set up correctly) both pass on his machine.
* Read the four project documents in this order and take notes: {{PoliSim_Development_Framework.docx}}, {{PoliSim_Engineering_Distinctions_Report.docx}}, {{PoliSim_Engineering_Handoff_Report.docx}}, {{PoliSim_Canonical_Data_Model_v0_2.docx}}. Ask questions about anything unclear; there are no stupid questions this week.
* Read {{CLAUDE.md}}, {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}, and the {{docs/adr/}} folder in full. These are the load-bearing documents for day-to-day work.
* Ship one small PR touching real code. Pick from any of: adding a test case to an under-tested module, fixing a typo or small clarity issue in documentation, tightening a type annotation, refactoring a small function for readability. Scope the PR to a single file if possible, two files maximum. The point is the workflow, not the change. Kevin will review and merge.

Done when

* Local stack is running and tests pass on Elijah's machine.
* All four project documents have been read.
* {{CLAUDE.md}}, {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}, and ADRs have been read.
* One PR is open, reviewed, and merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* None.

Out of scope

* Any ticket requiring architectural decisions, new schema, new endpoints, or non-trivial code changes. Those come in later once the landscape is familiar.
* Setting up any external integrations (cloud credentials, Anthropic API keys, monitoring accounts). Kevin handles those; Elijah does not need them for week-one work.

Priority:

High.

column1: SimulationRun and AgentOutput models and migration
column27:
{{SimulationRun}} is the lineage record for a single scenario execution; {{AgentOutput}} is the atomic structured reaction produced by one persona for one run. Per canonical data model §5.3 and §5.4, these two objects are always written together - a run without its outputs is incomplete, and an output without its parent run is uninterpretable. Both land in a single migration for that reason.

This is the largest and most schema-dense ticket in the Minimum-target batch because the traceability fields are load-bearing: they are what turns PoliSim from a prompt-in-prompt-out system into an auditable simulation platform. Every field in §5.3 and §5.4 is included, even where some will carry stubbed or placeholder values at Minimum scope.

Deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/simulation_run.py}}: {{SimulationRun}} model per canonical data model §5.3. All fields:
** {{id}}
** {{organization_id}} (FK, non-nullable)
** {{scenario_id}} (FK)
** {{scenario_version}}
** {{jurisdiction_feature_id}} (UUID, nullable, no FK constraint for now per {{Persona}} precedent)
** {{run_depth}} (enum: {{low | standard | deep}})
** {{active_persona_ids}} (UUID array)
** {{composition_prompt_id}} (UUID, nullable)
** {{simulation_prompt_id}} (UUID, nullable)
** {{evaluator_prompt_ids}} (UUID array, nullable)
** {{model_id}} (str)
** {{model_settings}} (JSONB)
** {{evidence_pack_ids}} (UUID array, nullable)
** {{status}} (enum: {{pending | running | complete | failed}})
** {{synthesis_output}} (JSONB, nullable)
** {{analyst_notes}} (text, nullable)
** {{created_at}}
** {{completed_at}} (nullable)
** {{created_by}}
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/agent_output.py}}: {{AgentOutput}} model per canonical data model §5.4 (Typed; {{mypy --strict}} clean). All fields:
** {{id}}
** {{organization_id}} (FK, non-nullable, denormalized from parent {{SimulationRun}} per §5.4)
** {{run_id}} (FK to {{simulation_runs.id}})
** {{persona_id}} (FK to {{personas.id}})
** {{persona_version}}
** {{dynamic_salience}} (JSONB, nullable)
** {{stance}} (enum: {{support | oppose | mixed | ignore | wait_and_see}})
** {{intensity}} (float, 0.0–1.0)
** {{trust_shift}} (float, -1.0–1.0)
** {{backlash_risk}} (float, 0.0–1.0)
** {{mobilization_likelihood}} (float, 0.0–1.0)
** {{message_resonance}} (JSONB)
** {{top_objections}} (str array)
** {{best_counterframes}} (str array)
** {{likely_public_language}} (str array)
** {{shareability_risk}} (float, 0.0–1.0)
** {{raw_llm_output}} (JSONB)
** {{evaluator_scores}} (JSONB, nullable)
** {{created_at}}
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0006_run_lineage.py}}: single migration creating both {{simulation_runs}} and {{agent_outputs}} tables, with FK from {{agent_outputs.run_id}} to {{simulation_runs.id}}. Both tables created and dropped atomically. Reversible.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/__init__.py}}: export both.
* {{backend/tests/unit/test_run_lineage_models.py}}: construction for both models, enum validation, float range constraints at the model level if available, JSONB round-tripping.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_run_lineage_migration.py}}: migration applies cleanly. Insert one {{SimulationRun}}, insert two {{AgentOutput}} rows linked to it, read back, assert FK relationships and field round-tripping. Confirm cascade or lack thereof on {{SimulationRun}} deletion matches spec (recommendation: no cascade; runs are historical records and should not be deletable in practice, but this ticket doesn't enforce that beyond the migration shape).

Done when

* {{make migrate}} applies {{0006_run_lineage}} cleanly.
* Unit and integration tests pass.
* {{alembic downgrade -1}} cleanly reverses, dropping both tables.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* AI-34 merged (Organization FK target).
* AI-55 merged ({{SimulationRun.scenario_id}} FK target).
* AI-56 merged ({{AgentOutput.persona_id}} FK target).

Out of scope

* {{PromptTemplate}}, {{EvidencePack}}, {{JurisdictionFeature}} models. Their FK columns exist on these models as nullable UUIDs without FK constraints for now. Follow-up migration adds the constraints when the parent models land.
* Evaluator logic. {{evaluator_prompt_ids}} and {{evaluator_scores}} fields exist; nothing populates them yet.
* Run orchestration logic. The models hold the shape; {{POST /simulate}} wires the writes.
* Soft-delete, archival, or retention policy on run records.
* Querying runs by jurisdiction, by persona, or by scenario beyond simple FK lookups.

Priority: High. Blocks {{POST /simulate}} and {{GET /runs/{id}}}. Largest of the Product Foundation model tickets; schedule time accordingly.
