01/May/26 8:41 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #46, squashed and merged to {{dev}}

Implements {{canonical-data-model.md}} §5.3 ({{SimulationRun}}) and §5.4 ({{AgentOutput}}) - the load-bearing per-run lineage objects.

What landed:

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/simulation_run.py}}: {{SimulationRun}} model with three name-qualified ENUMs ({{run_depth}}, {{simulation_run_status}}, {{agent_output_stance}}), inline {{created_at}} + {{completed_at}} (no {{TimestampMixin}}), all canonical §5.3 fields including deferred-FK columns ({{jurisdiction_feature_id}}, {{composition_prompt_id}}, {{simulation_prompt_id}}) and UUID/string array columns.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/agent_output.py}}: {{AgentOutput}} model with five {{Float}} columns (no DB CHECK constraints per canonical §9.2 deferred posture), three string-array columns with empty-array server defaults, two NOT NULL {{JSONB}} columns ({{message_resonance}}, {{raw_llm_output}}).
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0006_run_lineage.py}}: atomic migration creating both tables with strict tenant-only RLS via {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} on both, four {{NAMING_CONVENTION}} indexes, FK {{ondelete="RESTRICT"}} on {{agent_outputs.run_id}} → {{simulation_runs.id}}.
* {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}}: {{app_engine}} fixture promoted from {{test_scenario_rls.py}} per the rule-of-three convention ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] → [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] → [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link]). Scenario RLS tests still pass against the lifted fixture.
* 62 new tests across three files: 48 unit (column-metadata pins including {{values_callable}} defensiveness), 8 integration migration (full-field round-trip, ENUM lowercase storage, FK RESTRICT integrity), 6 integration RLS (covering ADR 0005 §Verification items 1, 2, 8 for both new tables).

Verification:

* {{alembic upgrade head}} / {{downgrade -1}} / {{upgrade head}} round-trip clean against fresh DB through {{0006_run_lineage}}.
* {{pytest}} unit + integration suites both clean (existing + new).
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean (39 files).
* Manual {{psql}} verification on the local stack confirmed: all 19 columns per table with correct types and nullability, four RLS policies per table using the {{NULLIF}} fail-closed pattern from ADR 0005 §3, FK {{ON DELETE RESTRICT}} visible on {{agent_outputs.run_id}}, all four indexes present with {{NAMING_CONVENTION}} names.

Out of scope per locked decisions, deferred to follow-ups:

* No route handlers ({{POST /simulate}}, {{GET /runs/{id}}}) - separate ticket.
* No repository-layer modules - tests interact with models via the two-engine pattern directly.
* No Pydantic v2 schema-level validators on float ranges - deferred to runtime / API work per canonical §9.2.
* No DB CHECK constraints on float ranges - same.
* No parent models for {{PromptTemplate}}, {{EvidencePack}}, {{JurisdictionFeature}} - deferred to [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link] and future tickets.
* No FK constraints on the deferred-FK UUID columns or array elements (Postgres doesn't natively support array-element FKs).

One pre-existing defect surfaced during manual verification: {{alembic downgrade base}} from {{0006}} fails on {{0002_enable_pgvector}}'s downgrade with {{must be owner of extension vector}}. Filed as [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-99|smart-link]. Operationally invisible; not introduced by this PR.

column1: Persona model, migration, and minimal seed
column27:
{{Persona}} is the stored, humanized profile derived from a composition stack (ideology base, state lens, overlays, jurisdiction context). Per canonical data model §4.1, a stored persona is never mutated at runtime - it becomes an agent only when a scenario is injected. This ticket lands the ORM model, the migration, and a minimal seed of one or two test-fixture personas sufficient to exercise the {{/simulate}} endpoint later.

Composition-stack references ({{ideology_base_id}}, {{state_lens_id}}, {{overlay_ids}}, {{jurisdiction_feature_id}}) are defined in the canonical model but the parent objects (IdeologyBase, StateLens, MicroOverlay, JurisdictionFeature) are out of Minimum-target scope. For now these FKs are declared as nullable columns with no FK constraint - the columns exist for schema compatibility, but no parent rows exist yet. When composition engine work lands post-5/4/2026, the FK constraints get added in a follow-up migration and the parent tables get populated.

Deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/persona.py}}: {{Persona}} SQLAlchemy 2.x declarative model per canonical data model §4.1. All fields from the spec: {{id}}, {{name}}, {{version}}, {{ideology_base_id}} (UUID, nullable, no FK constraint for now), {{state_lens_id}} (UUID, nullable, no FK constraint), {{overlay_ids}} (UUID array, nullable), {{jurisdiction_feature_id}} (UUID, nullable, no FK constraint), {{composition_weights}} (JSONB), {{age_band}}, {{life_stage}}, {{household_profile}}, {{occupation}}, {{education_level}}, {{housing_status}}, {{institutional_anchors}}, {{core_motivations}}, {{core_fears}}, {{issue_priorities}} (JSONB), {{media_habits}}, {{tone_profile}}, {{persuasion_triggers}}, {{backlash_triggers}}, {{engagement_style}}, {{created_at}}, {{is_active}}. Typed; {{mypy --strict}} clean.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0005_persona.py}}: creates {{personas}} table. Reversible.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/__init__.py}}: export {{Persona}}.
* {{backend/tests/unit/test_persona_model.py}}: construction, required vs nullable fields, JSONB field round-tripping.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_persona_migration.py}}: migration applies cleanly, one fixture persona inserts and reads back.
* Test fixture: one or two hardcoded {{Persona}} rows in {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}} representing a minimally-realistic composition (e.g. one Utah military-spouse persona, one urban progressive persona). These are test-only seed data, not production seeds. Sufficient for the {{POST /simulate}} endpoint to have something to reference.

Done when

* {{make migrate}} applies {{0005_persona}} cleanly.
* Unit and integration tests pass.
* At least one fixture persona is inserted in the integration test setup.
* {{alembic downgrade -1}} cleanly reverses without errors.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* AI-34 merged. Note: {{Persona}} is not currently Organization-scoped per the canonical data model. If that decision changes (Personas become tenant-scoped), an {{organization_id}} column gets added in a follow-up migration.

Out of scope

* {{IdeologyBase}}, {{StateLens}}, {{MicroOverlay}}, {{JurisdictionFeature}} models. Deferred until composition engine work begins post-May-4.
* FK constraints on composition-stack columns. Nullable columns only for now.
* Composition engine logic (selecting which stacks exist in a geography). Deferred.
* Persona derivation from a real composition stack. The fixture personas are hand-written for now.
* Production seed data. Test fixtures only.
* Any endpoint surfacing {{Persona}} CRUD.

Priority: Medium. Can run in parallel.
column53:
30/Apr/26 11:04 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #43, squash-merged to {{dev}}.

What landed

* New {{Persona}} ORM model ({{backend/src/polisim/db/models/persona.py}}) per canonical-data-model §4.1: 23 fields plus the three-mixin stack. System-level reference data - no {{organization_id}}, no RLS, no {{apply_tenant_only_policy}} call.
* Migration {{0005_create_personas}} chains after {{0004_create_scenarios}}. {{upgrade()}} creates the personas table with all 26 columns. {{downgrade()}} is single-line {{op.drop_table("personas")}} - no enum types, no auxiliary indexes.
* Composition-stack columns ({{ideology_base_id}}, {{state_lens_id}}, {{overlay_ids}}, {{jurisdiction_feature_id}}) declared as nullable UUIDs without FK constraints - parent tables are [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link] territory.
* JSONB column pattern established: {{composition_weights}} as {{Mapped[dict[str, float] | None]}}, {{issue_priorities}} as {{Mapped[list[dict[str, Any]] | None]}}. mypy --strict accepted both annotations on first try.
* ARRAY-of-UUID pattern: {{overlay_ids}} via {{postgresql.ARRAY(sa.Uuid())}} - mixed-dialect declaration with the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] generic {{Uuid}} posture.
* 27 unit tests in {{test_persona_model.py}} (column metadata, parametrized NOT NULL sweep across 14 fields, JSONB/ARRAY type checks, default pinning).
* 3 integration tests in {{test_persona_migration.py}} (full round-trip including JSONB/ARRAY values, server-side defaults, fixture lifecycle smoke test).
* Two hand-crafted persona fixtures in {{conftest.py}} (Layton military spouse, SLC progressive renter) on session scope. Stable hardcoded UUIDs ({{MILSPOUSE_PERSONA_ID}}, {{URBAN_PROGRESSIVE_PERSONA_ID}}) for downstream [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|smart-link] consumption. Composition-weights and issue-priorities both sum to 1.0 in both personas.

Verification

* mypy --strict clean (36 source files).
* 55 unit + 25 integration tests green locally.
* {{Backend — Integration Tests}} check ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link]) green on this PR - first PR to exercise CI integration coverage on a Persona-shaped surface.
* Manual psql round-trip: {{\\d personas}} shows correct column types, no FK constraints, no RLS policies; {{relrowsecurity = f}} on {{pg_class}}; {{pg_policies}} returns 0 rows; alembic upgrade/downgrade cycle is reversible.

Decisions held during execution

* Persona is NOT tenant-scoped (per §4.1). The §2 prose tension ("every object that holds customer-facing data carries organization_id") is real but §4.1 is authoritative for the Persona schema. Filed as a follow-up observation; folded into [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-92|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-92|smart-link]'s canonical-data-model sweep scope.
* JSONB over JSON: §4.1's "JSON" type is shape-not-column-type; the canonical data model is type-agnostic per §1, and JSONB is the standard Postgres column type for JSON-shaped data. Documented in both the model and migration docstrings.
* UUID convention going forward: {{sa.Uuid()}} everywhere new. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-96|smart-link] sweeps {{organization.py}} and {{scenario.py}} (scope corrected - both files use the dialect form, not just scenario.py).
* {{is_active}} posture: matches Organization exactly. {{version}} is Python-side {{default=1}} per scenario.py precedent.
* All humanization / psychological / behavior fields NOT NULL - [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] silent-defaults-to-NOT-NULL posture. Reference data should be fully populated at insert.
* Session-scoped fixture pattern (first in the codebase) - pytest-asyncio 1.3.0 supports {{loop_scope="session"}} natively; no version pin to pyproject.toml needed.
* Smoke-test consumer for the session-scoped fixture - without it, the fixture would be defined-but-dormant until [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] lands. The smoke test exercises setup/teardown lifecycle in CI from day one.

Notable execution-time observations

* Pre-commit's ruff-format auto-fixed two files on the first commit attempt (third datapoint after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link]). Workaround: {{git add -u}} and re-commit. This is the recurring pattern [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-97|smart-link] tracks; recommend bumping AI-97 priority before Elijah onboards Monday.
* mypy --strict accepted {{Mapped[dict[str, float] | None]}} for JSONB on first try. No fallback to {{dict[str, Any]}} needed. Useful precedent for any future JSONB column.
* AI-96 scope correction: original ticket named only scenario.py as the UUID-convention sweep target. Reading the codebase during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] confirmed organization.py also uses UUID(as_uuid=True). The AI-96 ticket description needs a small edit; will handle as part of post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] cleanup.

Patterns established

* System-level (non-tenant-scoped) reference-data table pattern: model + migration + tests, no RLS verification needed. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link]'s composition-layer models will follow the same shape.
* Composition-stack columns as nullable UUIDs without FK constraints - the right shape for a model whose parent tables are themselves deferred. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link] will retroactively constrain via {{op.create_foreign_key}} in its migration.
* JSONB and ARRAY-of-UUID column patterns - both work cleanly with {{Mapped[...]}} and mypy --strict. Future PromptTemplate ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|smart-link]) and other JSONB-bearing tables can copy these declarations directly.
* Session-scoped fixture for hardcoded reference data - first instance in the test harness. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|smart-link] will be the real consumers.

Unblocks

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] (SimulationRun + AgentOutput) - {{AgentOutput.persona_id}} has a real FK target now.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-60|smart-link] (POST /simulate orchestrator) - has stable persona IDs to consult via {{MILSPOUSE_PERSONA_ID}} / {{URBAN_PROGRESSIVE_PERSONA_ID}}.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link] (composition layer models) - Persona's composition-stack columns are ready to receive FK constraints once the parent tables land.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-77|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-77|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-84|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-84|smart-link] (Davis-Weber + UT-3 personas) - registry shape is in place; can populate via Alembic data migration or production-seed flow.

column1: Scenario model and migration
column27:
{{Scenario}} is the testable stimulus injected into the runtime agent engine - a policy proposal, message, messenger choice, event, or implementation detail that personas react to. Per canonical data model §5.1, every {{Scenario}} belongs to exactly one {{Organization}}; scenarios are tenant-scoped, not global.

This is the simplest of the runtime objects and serves as the pattern-setter for every subsequent tenant-scoped model (PersonaTemplate, SimulationRun, AgentOutput). Its Organization FK relationship and its tenant-scoping patterns are copied forward.

Deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/scenario.py}}: {{Scenario}} SQLAlchemy 2.x declarative model per canonical data model §5.1. Fields: {{id}} (UUID, PK), {{organization_id}} (UUID, FK to {{organizations.id}}, non-nullable), {{name}} (str), {{version}} (int, default 1), {{scenario_type}} (enum: {{policy | message | messenger | event | implementation_detail}}), {{content}} (text), {{messenger}} (text, nullable), {{implementation_notes}} (text, nullable), {{created_at}} (timestamp), {{created_by}} (str). Typed; mypy --strict clean.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0004_scenario.py}}: Alembic migration chained to {{0003_organization}}. Creates {{scenarios}} table with FK to {{organizations.id}}. Reversible: downgrade drops the table.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/__init__.py}}: export {{Scenario}}.
* {{backend/tests/unit/test_scenario_model.py}}: construction, enum validation, default version, required fields.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_scenario_migration.py}}: applies migrations, inserts a {{Scenario}} scoped to the test Organization fixture, reads it back, asserts {{organization_id}} round-trips correctly. Same throwaway-engine pattern as {{test_pgvector.py}} and {{test_organization_migration.py}}.

Done when

* {{make migrate}} applies {{0004_scenario}} cleanly against a fresh DB.
* Unit and integration tests pass.
* {{alembic downgrade -1}} cleanly reverses {{0004}} without errors.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] merged (Organization table and FK target exist).

Out of scope

* Any Scenario CRUD endpoint. The model and migration are this ticket's scope; endpoints come with POST /simulate.
* Scenario templates, seed library, or pre-built test scenarios. A test-only fixture is sufficient; production content is a separate concern.
* Scenario versioning beyond the {{version}} integer field. Semantic version-bumping logic on content change is a later concern.
* Linking Scenario to PromptTemplate. Deferred to Minimum Viable Runtime.

Priority:

Medium. Can run in parallel with others.
column53:
30/Apr/26 8:57 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #40, merged to {{dev}}.

What landed:

* {{Scenario}} ORM model ({{backend/src/polisim/db/models/scenario.py}}) per canonical-data-model §5.1: tenant-scoped via {{organization_id}} FK to {{organizations.id}}, native Postgres ENUM {{scenario_type}} with {{values_callable}} for lowercase SQL labels, {{version}} defaulting to 1, {{created_by}} as free-text {{String(255)}}, {{messenger}} and {{implementation_notes}} nullable {{Text}}. Three-mixin pattern ({{Base}}, {{UUIDPKMixin}}, {{TimestampMixin}}) matches [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]'s Organization precedent.
* Migration {{0004_create_scenarios}} chains after {{0003_create_organizations}}. {{upgrade()}} creates the table, the single-column index {{ix_scenarios_organization_id}}, and calls {{apply_tenant_only_policy("scenarios")}} bare (not wrapped in {{op.execute(...)}}). {{downgrade()}} drops the index, the table, and the {{scenario_type}} enum type.
* First migration to call a helper from {{polisim.db.policy_helpers}} - establishes the pattern [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] and downstream tenant-scoped tables will copy.
* 15 unit tests in {{test_scenario_model.py}} (construction, attribute round-trip, enum membership, column metadata).
* 5 integration tests in {{test_scenario_rls.py}} covering ADR 0005 §Verification items 1, 2, 7, 8 against the real {{scenarios}} table - first per-table RLS verification at the product layer.

Verification:

* mypy --strict clean.
* 28 unit + 22 integration tests green (15 + 5 new, all pre-existing untouched).
* Manual psql round-trip via {{docker compose exec db psql}} confirmed: no GUC returns zero rows, GUC = org A returns A's row only, INSERT with foreign org_id raises SQLSTATE 42501.
* CI green on the unit suite (integration suite still local-only pending [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-31|smart-link]).

Decisions held during execution:

* Skipped required-field-enforcement unit test (would be flush-time integration coverage; [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] precedent).
* Skipped {{CHECK (version >= 1)}} constraint (match canonical model literally).
* Duplicated the {{app_engine}} fixture in the new test file rather than hoisting to conftest.py (rule of three - [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] was caller #1, AI-55 is #2; the third caller hoists).
* Hand-constructed psql verification via {{docker compose exec db psql}} rather than adding a {{make psql}} target (that's [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-94|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-94|smart-link]'s territory).

Notable execution-time observations:

* Pre-commit's ruff-format auto-fixed one line on the first commit attempt (same pattern caught on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link]). {{make hooks}} ran clean pre-staging; the on-commit run picked up an additional auto-fix. Bench-noted; if it recurs on AI-56 / AI-57 we tighten CC's working-pattern verification step.
* The model uses {{UUID(as_uuid=True)}} (dialect-specific) for {{organization_id}} while the migration uses {{sa.Uuid()}} (generic). Both produce {{uuid}} Postgres columns. Worth picking one convention before [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] / [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] introduce more UUID FKs.

Patterns established for downstream tenant-scoped tables:

* Migration-helper-call pattern: bare {{apply_tenant_only_policy("<table>")}} at the end of {{upgrade()}}, after the table and index are created.
* Per-table RLS verification: 5-test integration file covering items 1, 2, 7, 8 with the two-engine fixture pattern ({{migrations_engine}} + local {{app_engine}}).
* Test-file module docstring: name which §Verification items the file covers and which are deliberately NOT covered (with reasons).

Unblocks: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] (Persona) and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] (SimulationRun + AgentOutput) - both will copy the patterns above.

column1: Tenancy resolution: implement get_current_org() and enforce org-scoped queries'
column27:
Canonical data model §8 requires that no query on tenant-scoped data execute without an {{organization_id}} filter, enforced at the application layer. A stub {{get_current_org()}} exists in {{backend/src/polisim/tenancy/}} (or equivalent; verify during context-load) that raises {{NotImplementedError}}. This ticket replaces the stub with a real header-based resolver suitable for MVP and wires it into the request dependency chain.

Authentication is deferred - at this stage, {{X-Org-Slug}} in the request header is sufficient to identify the tenant. Real auth (SSO, API tokens, session-based) comes later. The resolver must fail closed: absent or unknown {{X-Org-Slug}} returns 401, not a default tenant.

This ticket is the second pattern-setter after [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]. Every subsequent endpoint ticket (POST /simulate, GET /runs/{id}) inherits the dependency pattern established here.

Deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/tenancy/resolver.py}} (or update existing stub location): real {{get_current_org()}} FastAPI dependency that reads {{X-Org-Slug}} from request headers, queries {{Organization}} by slug, returns the row. Raises {{HTTPException(401, "Organization not found")}} if the slug is missing or doesn't match an active {{Organization}}.
* FastAPI dependency injection: {{get_current_org}} is importable from a stable location ({{polisim.deps}} or {{polisim.tenancy.resolver}}, whichever matches existing conventions) and usable as {{Depends(get_current_org)}} in route handlers.
* At least one test route wired to the dependency (can be a temporary {{/api/v1/_tenancy_probe}} or similar; will be removed when real endpoints land, or kept as a healthcheck extension). Return something trivial like {{{"org_slug": org.slug}}}.
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_tenancy_resolution.py}}: tests covering (1) happy path with valid slug, (2) missing header → 401, (3) unknown slug → 401, (4) inactive org ({{is_active=False}}) → 401. Uses the Organization fixture from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link].
* Cross-tenant isolation test: create two Organizations in the fixture, issue requests with each slug, confirm each response is scoped to the correct org. This test is load-bearing - it's the first proof that tenant isolation actually works in practice.

Done when

* {{get_current_org()}} raises no {{NotImplementedError}}.
* Integration tests pass, including the cross-tenant isolation test.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] merged (Organization model exists).

Out of scope

* Authentication (SSO, API tokens, sessions).
* RBAC beyond org scoping (per-user roles inside an org).
* Rate limiting or quota enforcement per org.
* Audit logging of tenant resolution events (separate concern).
* Default-tenant fallback behavior. Fail closed only.
* Query-layer enforcement of {{organization_id}} filters in ORM code - that's a convention the model tickets will each uphold; this ticket only handles request-time resolution.

Priority:

High. Blocks all endpoints.
column53:
30/Apr/26 8:10 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #39, squash-merged to {{dev}}.

What landed:

* Resolver: {{polisim.tenancy.resolver.get_current_org}} reads X-Org-Slug, looks up Organization via new {{OrganizationRepository.get_by_slug}}, returns it. All three failure modes (missing header, unknown slug, inactive org) return HTTP 401 with generic body; structured logs distinguish reason codes.
* Empty-string header treated as semantically equivalent to missing ({{if not x_org_slug:}}), preventing misleading {{unknown_slug}} log entries.
* Dependency rename {{get_db}} → {{get_tenant_session}}, per ADR 0005 Decision 8. New dependency calls {{set_config('app.current_org_id', $1, true)}} inside the request transaction before yielding, matching ADR 0005 §4 reference Python.
* Carve-out {{get_unscoped_session}} per Decision 13 - only legitimate non-tenant session in the codebase, used by the resolver itself and by /readyz.
* Probe route {{GET /api/v1/_tenancy_probe}} exercises both dependencies in a single request and reads back current_setting() to prove end-to-end wiring.
* Repository pattern precedent established for [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link]+.

Verification:

* mypy --strict clean.
* 13 unit + 17 integration tests green (6 new tenancy resolution tests + 11 pre-existing).
* Manual psql round-trip confirmed per the post-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] convention: two curls with two slugs round-trip their own org_id as guc_value; fresh polisim_app psql session shows empty current_setting() - no GUC leak across pooled connections.

Notable execution-time deviations from the plan:

* Pre-commit caught two B008 errors (Depends-in-default) on the initial commit attempt. Fixed by switching {{get_tenant_session}} and {{get_current_org}} to the modern {{Annotated[Type, Depends(...)]}} form. Pattern now consistent across the new modules.
* Empty-string header bug caught during manual log verification: the predicate guard initially shipped as {{if x_org_slug is None:}} and the empty header was falling through to the slug-lookup branch. Fixed pre-commit; new test added with structlog.testing.capture_logs to lock the behavior.
* Late-import workaround in {{api/deps.py}} to break the deps↔resolver cycle. Works but is structural debt; tracked as a follow-up below.

Follow-ups filed (Low priority, post-merge):

* Move get_unscoped_session from deps.py to db/session.py to break the cycle structurally and remove the late-import workaround.
* DX: make psql target + operator doc for postgresql+psycopg:// scheme; document host-side env override for running integration tests outside the compose network.
* Test fixture factory for Organization to dedupe the three near-identical INSERT patterns now in the suite.

Unblocks: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] , and every endpoint ticket downstream.

column1: Reconcile detect-secrets vs gitleaks references in project documentation
column27:
Project documentation contains inconsistent references to the active secret-scanning tool in pre-commit.

Actual state: {{.pre-commit-config.yaml}} uses {{gitleaks}} (version 8.30.1) as the secret-scanning hook. This is the tool that runs locally on every commit and in CI via the {{pre-commit}} job.

Drift: [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|smart-link]'s squash commit message ({{ccae7fe}}) references "detect-secrets" as the scanner, which is a different tool. The commit message is a historical artifact that can't be rewritten without force-pushing {{dev}} (not worth the cost), but other references to "detect-secrets" may exist in documentation or tickets and should be updated to match reality.

Discovered during {{2026-04-22}} board-wide review.

Deliverables

* Grep the repo for "detect-secrets" references: {{grep -ri "detect-secrets" .}} (excluding {{.git/}} and {{node_modules/}}).
* Update any live documentation (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/, ADRs) that references detect-secrets to reference gitleaks instead.
* Leave the commit message as-is (historical record, not worth a force-push).
* Optionally: note the drift in a short comment in {{.pre-commit-config.yaml}} or in ADR-0001 if the provenance matters.

Done when

* No "detect-secrets" references remain in live documentation.
* Any found references have been corrected to "gitleaks" or removed as appropriate.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* None.

Out of scope

* Switching the actual tool from gitleaks to detect-secrets (or vice versa). This ticket is purely a documentation reconciliation.
* Rewriting the historical commit message in {{ccae7fe}}..

Priority:

Low. Doc drift.
column53:
01/May/26 11:47 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Closed as a no-op. Verified on {{dev}} @ {{6d670c0}}.

Verification:

* {{grep -rin "detect-secrets" . --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.venv --exclude-dir=__pycache__}} - zero hits.
* {{grep -rin -E "detect[_-]secrets?" .}} (case-insensitive variant catching {{Detect-Secrets}}, {{DETECT_SECRETS}}, etc.) - zero hits.
* {{.pre-commit-config.yaml:27-30}} confirms {{gitleaks v8.30.1}} as the only configured secret scanner.

Surviving references to {{detect-secrets}} exist only in three historical commit message bodies (release-promotion PR descriptions), which are immutable and explicitly out of scope per the ticket.

One contradiction with the ticket description: AI-15's commit {{ccae7fe}} does not reference {{detect-secrets}} - its message already says {{gitleaks v8.30.1}}. The historical references are in subsequent release-promotion commits, not AI-15's. Doesn't change the outcome but flagging for the audit trail.

Drift was already resolved by prior work.

column1: Update docs/environments.md to reflect two-env-file split
column27:
{{docs/environments.md}} line 29 lists {{infra/.env}} as the secrets source for the local environment row. Accurate pre-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|smart-link] but now incomplete. Host-side Python processes ({{uv run alembic}}, {{pytest -m integration}}, direct {{uvicorn}} runs) also consume {{backend/.env}} with a different {{DATABASE_URL}} (localhost hostname instead of the compose network's {{db}} hostname, same {{+asyncpg}} driver scheme). The environments taxonomy doc should reflect both files.

Discovered during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|smart-link] investigation on {{2026-04-22}}. Deferred from [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|smart-link] scope because {{environments.md}} is a different concern (environment taxonomy doc, not developer setup README) and should consider how the host/container env-file split applies across all environments, not just local.

Deliverables

* Update the "local" row's Secrets source line to mention both files ({{infra/.env}} for compose, {{backend/.env}} for host tooling) with a one-line summary of which processes read each.
* Explicitly state in the doc whether the host/container env-file split applies to dev/staging/prod rows too, or whether it's a local-dev concern only. Most likely local-only (deployed environments don't run host-tooling processes the same way), but the doc should say so rather than leave it implicit.
* Point at {{backend/.env.example}} and {{infra/.env.example}} as the per-file templates.
* Keep the file's existing structure and environment-taxonomy framing intact.

Done when

* Local row reflects both env files and their separate purposes.
* Dev/staging/prod rows explicitly confirm whether the split applies.
* Reader can understand the host/container env-file distinction from {{environments.md}} alone, without needing to cross-reference the README.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|smart-link] merged (introduces {{backend/.env}}).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-44|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-44|smart-link] merged (locks the {{+asyncpg}} driver scheme for {{infra/.env}}).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|smart-link] merged (establishes the README's wording for the host/container split, which {{environments.md}} should stay consistent with).

Out of scope

* Any code or infra changes.
* {{README.md}} ( [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|smart-link] handles it).
* Restructuring the environments taxonomy itself.
* Consolidating to a single env file (two-file split is deliberate, locked in by ADR 0002).

Priority:

Low. Doc polish.
column53:
07/May/26 6:45 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Completed via PR #60, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

Closed the gap that left {{docs/environments.md}} naming only {{infra/.env}} in the local row's Secrets source cell. After [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|smart-link] introduced {{backend/.env}} for host-side Python tooling, the cell was incomplete: today, {{infra/.env}} is read by docker compose (the {{db}}, {{api}}, {{web}} services pick up {{DATABASE_URL}} with the {{db}} hostname) and {{backend/.env}} is read by host tooling ({{uv run alembic}}, {{pytest -m integration}}, host {{uvicorn}}) against {{localhost}}. The README and {{infra/README.md}} already documented this split via [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-46|smart-link]; {{docs/environments.md}} was the last authoritative environment doc that didn't.

Changes shipped:

* Local row Secrets source: rewritten to name both env files with their reading processes. Pointers to {{infra/.env.example}} and {{backend/.env.example}} are inline in the cell - no separate templates subsection was added (out of scope; the local row is the natural insertion point).
* Dev/staging/prod rows: each Secrets source cell gains a one-line clarifier stating that the local host/container env-file split does not apply to that environment. Phrasing is "from the backend" in the dev row (resolves to "dedicated secrets backend" earlier in that cell) and "from the secrets backend" in staging and prod (which lack the dev row's lead-in; "backend" is overloaded in this project - {{backend/}}, {{backend/.env}}, FastAPI service - so the explicit form removes ambiguity).

Local-only judgment supported by three pieces of evidence already in the file:

# The non-local Secrets source cells already commit to a secrets-backend pattern (Vault, cloud secrets manager, or equivalent) with per-environment isolation. That model injects credentials into running services directly - it doesn't surface as a host-vs-container file split.
# Progression rules §2/§3 frame "own secrets" and "own database" at the environment level, not at the process-class level. The host/container split is a local-development artifact (Python on the host, Postgres in a container, sharing one machine).
# Deployed environments don't run host-side {{uv run alembic}} against prod - migrations run as a deploy step inside CI/CD or a dedicated job. No second process class needing its own env file.

Outcome: a reader of {{docs/environments.md}} can now grasp the host/container distinction without cross-referencing the README.

Files changed: {{docs/environments.md}} (1 file, 4 cell-content replacements across four rows).

Out of scope (unchanged from ticket): {{README.md}}, {{infra/README.md}}, taxonomy restructuring, code or infra changes.

column1: Makefile wrappers for full app-profile stack and api logs
column27:
Context

Current Makefile targets (make dev, make down, make logs) wrap only the db-only development path. Bringing up the full api+db stack requires manually cd-ing into infra/ and remembering the README-documented command {{docker compose --profile apps up -d  --build db api}} - which intentionally omits web since web is pending AI-20. The long command is easy to mistype (e.g., omitting the service names and letting web crash-loop). Validation sessions and future debugging sessions would benefit from a one-word wrapper.

Discovered during [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|smart-link] deferred compose validation on {{2026-04-22}} - manual run used bare {{docker compose --profile apps up}}, hit web crash-loop noise for no real reason.

Deliverables

* Add {{make up}} (or rename {{make dev}} if preferred) that brings up db + api via the documented apps-profile command from repo root, no manual cd required.
* Add {{make logs-api}} that tails the api service specifically.
* Add {{make logs-all}} that tails every running service.
* Keep the existing db-only targets available under clear names (e.g., {{make up-db}}) - solo developers iterating on schema work should still have the light-weight path.
* Update infra/README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md to reference the wrappers as the primary commands; raw docker compose commands become the fallback.

Done when

* {{make up}} from repo root brings up db + api, wait for healthy, same end state as the documented manual command.
* {{make logs-api}} streams api logs without manual cd.
* Documentation updated.
* PR merged to dev.

Dependencies

* None.

Out of scope

* Adding web to any wrapper. That happens when [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link] makes web runnable.
* Any change to the compose file itself - this ticket is Makefile + docs only.

Priority:

Low. Polish.
column53:
05/May/26 1:52 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #55, squashed and merged to {{dev}}. 

Three additive Makefile targets ({{make dev-apps}}, {{make logs-api}}, {{make logs-all}}), the {{make down}} recipe gained {{--profile apps}} to symmetrically tear down what {{dev-apps}} brings up, the {{make dev}} help text was tightened, and the bring-up + log-tail prose in root README and infra/README pivoted to lead with the make wrappers.

Four in-flight diagnoses landed during plan vetting and smoke-test verification - full detail in the PR commit body, summarized here:

# Plan-vet: root README L72 {{docker compose ps}} would have been orphaned by the L62-64 pivot; fixed inline by prefixing with {{cd infra &&}}.
# Smoke-test: {{make logs-all}} originally specced as {{docker compose logs -f}} only tailed db (compose profile-gating filters out profiled services). Fixed by adding {{--profile apps}}.
# Smoke-test: same profile-gating bug in {{make down}}, newly user-visible because {{dev-apps}} brings up profiled services. Without the fix, api+web would have leaked after {{make down}}. Fixed by adding {{--profile apps}}.
# Manual user-replay in fresh terminal: root README L67 paragraph orphaned by the L62-74 pivot (referenced "a bare {{docker compose up}}" that no longer appeared on the page). Rewrote inline; took the [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link]-era "until backend and frontend are scaffolded" tail with it because leaving stale prose in a paragraph this PR was rewriting would have been a deliberate wrong choice. Other [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-20|smart-link]-era staleness on root README L11/L15/L85-86 stays [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-101|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-101|smart-link]'s territory.

Reviewer flagged a clarifying question on whether {{dev-apps}} was intended to run migrations (architecturally no - migrations stay an explicit separate step); the conversation surfaced a docs gap that's tracked as a follow-up ticket: a fresh-clone reader following root README's "Run the local stack" section is not currently told {{make migrate}} is required between {{make dev}} and {{make dev-apps}}.

Supersedes [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-103|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-103|smart-link] (closed earlier today as superseded - the narrower {{make dev-apps}}-only fix was absorbed into [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-43|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-43|smart-link]'s expanded scope).

column1: Document branch-protection-as-source-of-truth principle in `CONTRIBUTING.md`
column27:
[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-29|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-29|smart-link] surfaced a class of bug where governance docs describe rules that contradict what branch protection actually enforces (e.g., {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} claimed {{dev → main}} used merge commits, but “Require linear history” on {{main}} made that impossible). The root cause is that docs were written before protection was configured, and wasn’t revisited when it was.

To prevent recurrence, {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} should explicitly state that branch protection is the authoritative source of truth for governance, and any governance change starts with updating branch protection, then updating the docs to match- not the other way around. A PR that changes governance language without touching protection settings should be pushed back on.

Deliverables

* New short section in {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} (probably under “Pull request flow” or as its own section near the top of governance discussion) stating the principle in two or three sentences.
* Concrete guidance: when proposing a governance change, update branch protection first, then update {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}, then update {{CLAUDE.md}} and {{docs/framework.md}} if they reference the affected rule.
* Note for PR reviewers: a doc-only governance change is a yellow flag that should prompt “did you actually update branch protection?”

Done when

* Principle documented in {{CONTRIBUTING.md}}.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* None.

Out of scope

* Any actual governance changes. This ticket documents the meta-principle only.

Priority:

Low. Polish.
column53:
01/May/26 12:34 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #49, squashed and merged to {{dev}}.

New top-level section {{## Branch protection is the source of truth}} added to {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} between {{## Pull request flow}} and {{## Code review expectations}}. Two-sentence principle statement plus two bullets - concrete ordering for governance changes (protection first, then docs, then {{CLAUDE.md}} / {{docs/framework.md}} if they reference the affected rule) and a reviewer note flagging doc-only governance changes as a yellow flag.

Verification: {{make precommit}} clean on two consecutive runs, no auto-fix triggered. Section reads as a sibling of the surrounding governance sections, not a subsection.

No edits to {{CLAUDE.md}}, {{docs/framework.md}}, branch-protection settings, or PR templates - explicit out-of-scope per the ticket. The principle added is a meta-rule about how to make rule changes; it doesn't itself "affect" a specific rule.

No follow-ups.

column1: Organization model and migration
column27:
PoliSim is a commercial multi-tenant SaaS platform per the canonical data model §8. Every piece of customer-facing data (scenarios, simulation runs, agent outputs, client-provided evidence, jurisdiction assignments) is scoped to exactly one {{Organization}}. Multi-tenancy is a structural property of the data model, not an access-control layer applied on top.

This ticket lands the {{Organization}} ORM model, its Alembic migration, and the supporting test fixtures. It is the pattern-setter for every subsequent model ticket - Scenario, PersonaTemplate, SimulationRun, and AgentOutput will all inherit the Organization FK shape and the testing patterns established here.

Prior scope of this ticket ("Document first real-schema migration pattern") was superseded by [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|smart-link]'s completion, which established the migration pattern via {{0001_baseline}}. Rescoped {{2026-04-23}}.

Deliverables

* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/organization.py}}: {{Organization}} SQLAlchemy 2.x declarative model per canonical data model §8.1. Fields: {{id}} (UUID, PK), {{name}} (str), {{slug}} (str, unique, URL-safe), {{assigned_jurisdiction_ids}} (UUID array, nullable; empty list default), {{data_handling_tier}} (enum: {{public_only | includes_licensed | includes_client_provided}}), {{allow_training_use}} (bool, default False), {{created_at}} (timestamp), {{is_active}} (bool, default True). Typed; mypy --strict clean.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/migrations/versions/0003_organization.py}}: Alembic migration chained to {{0002_enable_pgvector}}. Creates {{organizations}} table with columns matching the model. Reversible: downgrade drops the table.
* {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/__init__.py}}: export {{Organization}}. If the file doesn't exist, create it.
* {{backend/tests/unit/test_organization_model.py}}: unit tests for model construction, slug uniqueness constraint at the model level, default values, enum validation. No DB connection required (construction-only).
* {{backend/tests/integration/test_organization_migration.py}}: integration test that applies migrations, inserts one {{Organization}} row, reads it back, asserts fields round-trip correctly. Uses the same throwaway-engine pattern as {{test_pgvector.py}}.
* Seed fixture: one test-only {{Organization}} row for use in later integration tests. Place in {{backend/tests/integration/conftest.py}} as a fixture, not in production seed data.

Done when

* {{make migrate}} applies {{0003_organization}} cleanly against a fresh DB.
* Unit tests pass in the default {{pytest}} run.
* Integration test passes under {{pytest -m integration}}.
* {{alembic downgrade -1}} cleanly reverses {{0003}} without errors.
* {{mypy --strict src/}} clean.
* PR merged to {{dev}}.

Dependencies

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-11|smart-link] merged (Alembic wired).
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-12|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-12|smart-link] merged (pgvector enabled, establishes the two-migration chain).

Out of scope

* Tenancy resolution ({{get_current_org()}}) - separate ticket. This ticket adds the model; the next ticket wires it into request handling.
* Any other canonical model object (Scenario, PersonaTemplate, SimulationRun, AgentOutput, IdeologyBase, StateLens, MicroOverlay, JurisdictionFeature, PromptTemplate, EvidencePack).
* RBAC, SSO, authentication beyond the org-slug resolution pattern.
* EULA text for {{allow_training_use}} - legal review pending; field exists but product flow doesn't set it yet.
* Seed data for production. Only test fixtures in this ticket.
* API endpoints referencing Organization - come with the endpoint tickets.

Priority:

High. Pattern-setter, blocks 4 others.
column53:
29/Apr/26 10:50 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Resolved via PR #36, merged to {{dev}}.

Organization model and migration shipped - the tenant root every other tenant-scoped table will FK to. Lands the ORM model at {{backend/src/polisim/db/models/organization.py}} per canonical-data-model §8.1, the Alembic migration {{0003_create_organizations.py}}, the {{seed_organization}} integration fixture, 8 unit tests, and 3 integration tests covering full-field round-trip (with two-UUID array order preservation), slug uniqueness via {{IntegrityError}}, and server-side default application.

Conventions established here for downstream models (Persona, Scenario, SimulationRun, AgentOutput):

* Tenant-scoped tables inherit {{Base, UUIDPKMixin, TimestampMixin}}.
* Status / classification enums use {{enum.StrEnum}} mapped via SQLAlchemy {{Enum(..., native_enum=True, create_type=True, values_callable=lambda x: [e.value for e in x])}}. The {{values_callable}} is load-bearing - without it, Postgres ENUM labels default to uppercase member names, contradicting the snake_case spec values.
* Migrations registering native ENUM types must include {{op.execute("DROP TYPE IF EXISTS <name>")}} in {{downgrade()}} because Alembic autogen drops the table but not the type.
* Models register on {{Base.metadata}} via a one-line {{from polisim.db import models  # noqa: F401}} in {{migrations/env.py}}. Localized side effect; {{polisim.db}} package import does not load every model.

Per ADR 0005 Decision 13, {{organizations}} is intentionally NOT RLS-protected. Migration docstring cites Decision 13 explicitly so future readers do not "fix" this. Every tenant-scoped child table will be RLS-protected when those land.

Deliberately deferred per [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] plan:

* Role provisioning ({{polisim_app}}, {{polisim_migrations}}) and {{infra/docker/postgres/init.sql}} → follow-up ticket before [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link].
* Two-URL config ({{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}}) and {{env.py}} rewrite per ADR 0005 Decision 14 → same follow-up.
* Helper module ({{apply_tenant_only_policy}}, {{apply_tenant_or_system_policy}}) → same follow-up; first concrete caller is [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link].
* {{get_current_org()}} → [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link].
* Pydantic schemas and slug format validation → API ticket.
* Postgres service container + integration test job in CI → separate ticket (integration tests run locally only today).
* mypy --strict in CI → separate ticket (currently local-only; pre-commit does not run it).
* ORM-only vs Postgres-trigger posture for {{updated_at}} → project-level decision ticket. Raw SQL UPDATEs bypass SQLAlchemy's {{onupdate}} hook by design; documented for deliberate resolution.
* {{process_revision_directives}} hook for auto-sequential migration numbering → low-priority sweep ticket.

Mid-execution amendments to the locked plan, both made with explicit approval and now locked at the project level for future enum mappings: {{(str, enum.Enum)}} → {{enum.StrEnum}} (ruff UP042 flagged the pre-3.11 idiom on Python 3.12); added {{values_callable}} to the {{Enum}} mapping to keep Postgres labels snake_case at runtime.

Unblocks [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link].

column1: Configure CI integration-test job with Postgres service container
column27:
PoliSim's integration tests ({{tests/integration/}}, marked with {{pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration}}) hit a real Postgres and currently run only on developer machines. CI runs unit tests only because no Postgres service container is provisioned. This is fine while integration tests are limited to schema round-trip ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link] - Organization), but blocks every subsequent canonical-data-model ticket that needs CI verification - especially [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] (Scenario, first RLS-protected table), where the 10-case verification spine from ADR 0005 §Verification cannot run in CI without it.

This ticket adds a CI job that provisions Postgres 16 + pgvector, runs migrations, runs the integration test suite, and reports as a separate required check on every PR.

Deliverables

* New job in {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}}: {{Backend — Integration Tests}}. Uses {{services:}} to provision {{pgvector/pgvector:pg16}}. Healthcheck-gated startup. Same Postgres credentials pattern as {{infra/.env.example}}.
* The job runs migrations (per the current {{make migrate}} pattern, or whatever [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] has reshaped that into), then {{cd backend && uv run pytest -m integration tests/integration/}}.
* If [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] (role provisioning) has merged by the time this ticket lands, the CI job provisions both {{polisim_app}} and {{polisim_migrations}} roles and uses {{DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS}} for the migrate step. If [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] has not merged, the job runs as the bootstrap superuser (matches local-dev posture pre-[https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link]). Detect the current posture and adapt; do not block on [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link].
* Add {{Backend — Integration Tests}} to the {{dev}} and {{main}} Rulesets as a required check.
* Verify on the PR: integration tests run in CI, fail for the right reasons (induce a known failure once on a probe branch and confirm the job catches it), then revert.

Done when

* {{Backend — Integration Tests}} job runs on every PR.
* The job is required on both Rulesets.
* Integration tests pass in CI for a clean PR.
* Inducing a test failure (e.g. revert one assertion in {{test_organization_migration.py}} on a probe branch) causes the job to fail. Then revert the probe.

Dependencies

* None hard. [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-88|smart-link] (role provisioning) is a soft dependency - if merged, the CI job uses the role split; if not, runs as superuser.

Out of scope

* Adding mypy --strict to CI - separate ticket.
* Bumping deprecated Node 20 actions - [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-87|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-87|smart-link].
* Frontend integration tests - frontend test framework is TBD ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-33|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-33|smart-link]).
* Test parallelization across multiple Postgres containers - premature; revisit when integration suite > 1 minute.

Priority

High. Gates [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] RLS verification in CI.
column53:
30/Apr/26 9:58 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Shipped via PR #41, squash-merged to {{dev}}. Post-merge verification complete.

What landed

* New {{backend-integration-test}} job in {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}} (~70 lines).
* pgvector/pgvector:pg16 service container with bootstrap env matching {{infra/.env.example}}.
* Wait-loop step (30 iterations × 1s, counted form, per-attempt logging) as defense-in-depth against the gap between service health and bootstrap entrypoint completion.
* psql -f infra/docker/postgres/init.sql with PGPASSWORD step-env and {{-v ON_ERROR_STOP=1}}. No SQL duplication; init.sql remains canonical.
* Two-URL env (DATABASE_URL + DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS) on both the alembic step and the pytest step. Settings construction at module import requires both even though alembic only reads the migrations URL.
* Pytest invocation: {{cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/ -m integration -v}}. The {{-m integration}} flag overrides the {{addopts = "-m 'not integration'"}} exclusion in pyproject.toml.

Verification

* Local pre-commit: {{make hooks}} clean (check-yaml validates), wait-loop bash and psql {{ON_ERROR_STOP=1}} behavior verified against the local compose stack.
* Post-merge induced-failure probe (probe PR #42, closed without merging): {{raise AssertionError(...)}} added to {{test_readyz_returns_ok_when_db_reachable}}, observed {{Backend — Integration Tests}} fail in 44s with the induced AssertionError; revert restored green. Run links posted as comment on PR #41.
* Rulesets updated: {{Backend — Integration Tests}} added as required status check on both {{dev}} and {{main}} Rulesets.

Decisions held during execution

* Single source of truth for role provisioning: psql -f init.sql (option Q2(a) from context-load), not inline SQL or a custom Docker image.
* Localhost-based URLs in CI (port-forwarded service container), no {{polisim/config.py}} changes required.
* Manual Ruleset addition via GHEC web UI per ADR 0006 precedent - YAML cannot self-register a check as required.

Notable execution-time observations

* Pre-commit caught a B011 ruff violation ({{assert False}}) on the induced-failure probe. Workaround: use {{raise AssertionError("...")}} instead. Worth noting in any future induced-failure runbook.
* ADR 0005 §Negative line 210 references [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-34|smart-link]'s task prompt as the original carrier for this CI work. The work landed under AI-31 instead. Folded into AI-92's existing ADR 0005 textual-sweep scope.

Patterns established

* Three-step Postgres-service-container pattern (service block + wait-loop + psql init.sql) is now in-repo for any future CI job that needs a DB.
* Two-URL env requirement is now codified in a workflow file, not just in prose. Future CI jobs touching Python code that imports {{polisim.config}} must set both.
* Induced-failure verification protocol completed end-to-end (probe → fail → revert → green → close → evidence). The pattern can be reused for any future required-check addition.

Unblocks

* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-56|smart-link] (Persona) and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-57|smart-link] (SimulationRun + AgentOutput) will ship with CI integration coverage from day one.
* [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-76|smart-link], [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-80|smart-link], and any future tenant-scoped table tickets inherit the same coverage.
* The "## CI coverage gap" callout in [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-54|smart-link] and [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-55|smart-link] PR bodies is now historical, not active debt.

column1: Decide hosting for dev, staging, and prod environments
column27:
PoliSim needs hosting decisions for three environments (local is out of scope: runs on developer machines). The decision affects environment taxonomy (AI-8), deployment pipeline design, secrets management backend, CI/CD runner placement, network access model, and cost planning.

Options to evaluate:

* On-premises/internal vm cluster (internally-managed)
* Commercial cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) - standard
* GovCloud (AWS GovCloud, Azure Gov) - compliance-aligned
* Hybrid (dev in cloud, staging/prod on-prem)

Criteria:

* Data sensitivity tier per environment
* Compliance requirement
* Cost per environment
* Time-to-stand up

Priority:

High. Gates late-May pilot.

column1: Enable GitHub native secret scanning and push protection in GHEC
column27:
Original {{2026-04-17}} scoping called for both gitleaks (code-side scanning) and GitHub native (repo-level push protection and secret scanning alerts). The motivation was defense-context-appropriate: leaked credentials in a defense-adjacent codebase are a materially higher-consequence failure than in a consumer app, so layered detection is worth the configuration effort.

Gitleaks is already enforced via pre-commit ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|smart-link] landed gitleaks via pre-commit-hooks; pre-commit CI job runs on every PR), so the gitleaks half of this ticket is satisfied. Verified: {{.pre-commit-config.yaml}} contains the gitleaks hook, and the {{pre-commit}} job in {{.github/workflows/ci.yml}} runs on PRs to dev and main.

GitHub native secret scanning and push protection are GHEC settings-level toggles, not code changes. Those remain to be enabled.

Note: this ticket was briefly conflated with [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-15|smart-link]'s commit-message reference to "detect-secrets" (a different scanner). The pre-commit config uses gitleaks, not detect-secrets - the commit-message reference was imprecise and is tracked as a small documentation reconciliation follow-up.

Deliverables

* Enable "Push protection" in the repo's Settings → Code security → Secret scanning.
* Enable "Secret scanning" alerts in the same panel.
* Verify by checking that the repo's Security tab shows secret-scanning as enabled, and optionally test push protection by attempting to commit a known-fake pattern.
* Add a one-line note in CONTRIBUTING.md under the governance section confirming that gitleaks + GitHub native scanning are both active (so provenance is documented for future contributors and for Elijah's onboarding).

Done when

* Both GHEC toggles are on and verified active.
* CONTRIBUTING.md reflects the governance state.
* PR merged to dev (for the CONTRIBUTING.md change only — the toggles are admin actions outside git).

Dependencies

* None. GHEC admin access only.

Out of scope

* Adding scanners beyond gitleaks + GitHub native.
* Customizing which secret patterns are scanned.
* Reconciling the "detect-secrets" reference in AI-15's commit message (tracked separately).

Priority:

Medium.
column53: 22/Apr/26 12:48 PM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Rescoped {{2026-04-22}} after board-wide review. Gitleaks half was already satisfied by [https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|smart-link] (pre-commit hook + pre-commit CI job); scope is now limited to the GHEC-side admin toggles plus a one-line `CONTRIBUTING.md` update confirming both layers are active.
column54:
08/May/26 6:29 AM;712020:e96dc7dd-86fa-42ce-b5b7-c5f4ecd20e29;Closed via PR #64, merged to {{dev}}.

What shipped:

* GHEC Settings → Code security → Advanced Security:
** Secret Protection: enabled (umbrella).
** Push protection: enabled - blocks pushes containing detected provider or non-provider credential patterns at the remote.
** Non-provider patterns: enabled - extends scanning beyond known vendor patterns to catch generic high-entropy credentials.
** Prevent direct alert dismissals: enabled - dismissing an alert requires submitting a request for approval rather than a one-click dismiss.
** Validity checks: deliberately left disabled (would forward detected secrets to third-party validity-check endpoints - not appropriate for a defense-adjacent codebase).
** Scan for generic passwords (Copilot AI): deliberately left disabled (AI-driven scanning of repo contents not opted into at this stage).
* Modified {{CONTRIBUTING.md}} - new {{### Secret detection}} subsection peer to {{### Pre-commit hooks}} under {{## Local setup}}. Documents the three active layers (gitleaks at commit time, push protection at push time, native scanning across history) and the dismissal-approval posture.

Verification:

* Retroactive history scan completed clean (0 open, 0 closed secret-scanning alerts in the Security tab). Confirms the gitleaks pre-commit hook ([https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|https://kenaidefense.atlassian.net/browse/AI-13|smart-link]) has been catching credentials at commit time since it landed.
* {{make hooks}} and {{make precommit}} clean.
* Reviewed and approved by Elijah.

Layered detection now active: gitleaks (commit time, local) + GitHub native push protection (push time, server) + GitHub native secret scanning (history, server). Defense-context appropriate posture.

No follow-ups.
